"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Heisenberg uncertainty principle</b></span> says we <u style="""">cannot</u>&nbsp;know a particle's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""position"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""momentum"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> simultaneously."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Heisenberg uncertainty principle</b></span> says we <u style="""">cannot</u>&nbsp;know a particle's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">position</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">momentum</span> simultaneously.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Heisenberg&#x20;uncertainty&#x20;principle&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> says we <u style="""">cannot</u>&nbsp;know a particle's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">position</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">momentum</span> simultaneously."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">Heisenberg uncertainty principle</b></span> says we <u style="""">cannot</u>&nbsp;know a particle's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">position</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">momentum</span> simultaneously.

"Avogadro's number is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">6 x 10<sup style="""">23</sup></span> - representing the number of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">molecules in 1 mole</span> of something.

"<div>Converting Celsius → Farenheit...</div><div><br></div>Temp. (°F) = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;1&#x2E;8&#x28;T&#xB0;C&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&#x2B;&#x20;32&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>"	"<div>Converting Celsius → Farenheit...</div><div><br></div>Temp. (°F) = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(1.8(T°C&nbsp;+ 32)</span>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

1.8 is multiplied by (T<sup>*C</sup> + 32)</div>"
"<div>Converting Farenheit → Celsius...</div><div><br></div><div>Temp (°C) = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""0&#x2E;6&#x28;T&#xB0;F&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&#x2D;&#x20;32&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Converting Farenheit → Celsius...</div><div><br></div><div>Temp (°C) = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">0.6(T°F&nbsp;- 32)</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"Electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">closest</i></span> to the atom's <b>nucleus</b> have the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;lowest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[high/low]</span> <u>potential energies</u>."	"Electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">closest</i></span> to the atom's <b>nucleus</b> have the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">lowest</i></span> <u>potential energies</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Think of electrical potential.</div><div><br></div><img src=""c-atom_e1.gif""></div>"
"Electrons <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;furthest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[closest/furthest]</span> from the atom's <b>nucleus</b> have the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">highest</i></span> <u>potential energies</u>."	"Electrons <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">furthest</i></span> from the atom's <b>nucleus</b> have the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">highest</i></span> <u>potential energies</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"Electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">furthest</i></span> from the atom's <b>nucleus</b> have the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;highest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[high/low]</span> <u>potential energies</u>."	"Electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">furthest</i></span> from the atom's <b>nucleus</b> have the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">highest</i></span> <u>potential energies</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Think of electrical potential.</div><div><br></div><img src=""c-atom_e1.gif""></div>"
"Electrons with the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;highest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[highest/lowest]</span> <b style="""">energy shells</b> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">furthest</i></span> from the nucleus."	"Electrons with the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">highest</i></span> <b style="""">energy shells</b> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">furthest</i></span> from the nucleus.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Valence electrons</div><div>- This high energy is what makes valence electrons highly reactive (since energy is inversely proportional to stability).</div><div><br></div><img src=""c-atom_e1.gif""></div>"
"Electrons with the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">highest</i></span> <b style="""">energy shells</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;furthest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[furthest/closest]</span> from the nucleus."	"Electrons with the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">highest</i></span> <b style="""">energy shells</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">furthest</i></span> from the nucleus.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Valence electrons</div><div>- This high energy is what makes valence electrons highly reactive (since energy is inversely proportional to stability).</div><div><br></div><img src=""c-atom_e1.gif""></div>"
"Out of every electon shell, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;valence&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b style="""">electrons</b> experience the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weakest</i></span> pull from their own nucleus."	"Out of every electon shell, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">valence</b></span> <b style="""">electrons</b> experience the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weakest</i></span> pull from their own nucleus.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Hence why they can easily ""leave"" to bind with other atoms.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""c-atom_e1.gif""><br></div></div>"
"Out of every electon shell, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">valence</b></span> <b style="""">electrons</b> experience the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;weakest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[strongest/weakest]</span> pull from their own nucleus."	"Out of every electon shell, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">valence</b></span> <b style="""">electrons</b> experience the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weakest</i></span> pull from their own nucleus.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<div>Electrons move from their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;ground&#x20;state&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> to their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;excited&#x20;state&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> when they <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>absorb</i></span> <u>photons of light</u>.</div><div></div>"	"<div>Electrons move from their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">ground state</b></span> to their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">excited state</b></span> when they <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>absorb</i></span> <u>photons of light</u>.</div><div></div>

<div>-&nbsp;<b>c</b> = speed of light (3 x 10<sup>8</sup>).</div><div>- Could be plugged in to replace f for the energy of a photon (below):</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""528d4645de60e515d5484c043d040532.png""><span style=""color: rgb(34, 34, 34);""><br></span></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>c</b> = speed of light (3 x 10<sup>8</sup>).</div><div>- Could be plugged in to replace f for the energy of a photon (below):</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""528d4645de60e515d5484c043d040532.png""><span style=""color: rgb(34, 34, 34);""><br></span></div></div>"
"The <b>emission spectra</b>&nbsp;of an element is generated once their electrons move <i>to</i> their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;ground&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> states &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""release&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;specific&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;photons&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>emission spectra</b>&nbsp;of an element is generated once their electrons move <i>to</i> their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u style="""">ground</u></span> states &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">release&nbsp;<i>specific&nbsp;</i><u>photons</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><i>Different </i>atoms have <i>different </i>emission spectras because they have electrons with <i>unique </i>energy level arrangements.</div><div><br></div><img src=""wUMSGtEMTVughxknReGu_Emission_spectrum_H_annotated.png""></div>"
"<div>Elements can be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>identified</u></i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by their <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;emission&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;absorption&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>spectra</b>, since each has a unique set of orbital arrangements.</div>"	"<div>Elements can be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>identified</u></i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by their <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>emission</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>absorption</b></span> <b>spectra</b>, since each has a unique set of orbital arrangements.</div>

<div><img src=""wUMSGtEMTVughxknReGu_Emission_spectrum_H_annotated.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;emission&#x20;spectra&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is an atom's&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>unique&nbsp;</i>set of&nbsp;<u>colored photons</u></span> emitted based on its electron orbital arrangements.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>emission spectra</b></span> is an atom's&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>unique&nbsp;</i>set of&nbsp;<u>colored photons</u></span> emitted based on its electron orbital arrangements.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>When they fall to their ground states, they release photons of light.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""wUMSGtEMTVughxknReGu_Emission_spectrum_H_annotated.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>emission spectra</b></span> is an atom's&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;unique&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;set&#x20;of&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;colored&#x20;photons&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> emitted based on its electron orbital arrangements.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>emission spectra</b></span> is an atom's&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>unique&nbsp;</i>set of&nbsp;<u>colored photons</u></span> emitted based on its electron orbital arrangements.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>When they fall to their ground states, they release photons of light.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""wUMSGtEMTVughxknReGu_Emission_spectrum_H_annotated.png""><br></div></div>"
"In an H atom, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;Lyman&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b style="""">series</b> is the <u style="""">photon emission</u>&nbsp;when electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">fall down &amp;&nbsp;<i>land on&nbsp;</i>n=1</span> - emitting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">UV</span> light."	"In an H atom, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Lyman</b></span> <b style="""">series</b> is the <u style="""">photon emission</u>&nbsp;when electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">fall down &amp;&nbsp;<i>land on&nbsp;</i>n=1</span> - emitting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">UV</span> light.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Energy difference between n=1 and higher principle quantum numbers is large... emitting photons with high energy levels (hence UV).</div><div>- It <u>doesn't</u> matter which shell the excited electron was at - the only thing that matters is where it ended (n=1).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a4f18b49f5fe68eb40b74fd962f7c3b6.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""80b27b2c3bce88d071f97cf63a01ca90.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yv9NAo4oxLQ"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yv9NAo4oxLQ</a><br></div></div>"
"In an H atom, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Lyman</b></span> <b style="""">series</b> is the <u style="""">photon emission</u>&nbsp;when electrons <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""fall&#x20;down&#x20;&amp;amp&#x3B;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;land&#x20;on&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;n&#x3D;1"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - emitting <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""UV"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> light."	"In an H atom, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Lyman</b></span> <b style="""">series</b> is the <u style="""">photon emission</u>&nbsp;when electrons <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">fall down &amp;&nbsp;<i>land on&nbsp;</i>n=1</span> - emitting <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">UV</span> light.

<div>- Energy difference between n=1 and higher principle quantum numbers is large... emitting photons with high energy levels (hence UV).</div><div>- It <u>doesn't</u> matter which shell the excited electron was at - the only thing that matters is where it ended (n=1).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a4f18b49f5fe68eb40b74fd962f7c3b6.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""80b27b2c3bce88d071f97cf63a01ca90.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yv9NAo4oxLQ"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yv9NAo4oxLQ</a><br></div></div>"

"In an H atom, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b style="""">Balmer</b></span> <b style="""">series</b> is the <u style="""">photon emission</u>&nbsp;when electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">fall down &amp;&nbsp;<i>land on&nbsp;</i>n=2</span> - emitting <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">visible</span> light.

<div><div><div>- Energy difference between n=2 and higher principle quantum numbers is somewhat large... emitting photons with mid-level energy (hence visible light).</div><div>- It <u>doesn't</u> matter which shell the excited electron was at - the only thing that matters is where it ended (n=2).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a4f18b49f5fe68eb40b74fd962f7c3b6.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""80b27b2c3bce88d071f97cf63a01ca90.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yv9NAo4oxLQ"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yv9NAo4oxLQ</a></div></div></div></div>"

<script>
// Prevent original image from loading before mask
aFade = 50, qFade = 0;
var mask = document.querySelector('#io-overlay>img');
function loaded() {
    var original = document.querySelector('#io-original');
    original.style.visibility = ""visible"";
}
if (mask === null || mask.complete) {
    loaded();
} else {
    mask.addEventListener('load', loaded);
}
</script>

<script>
// Toggle answer mask on clicking the image
var toggle = function() {
  var amask = document.getElementById('io-overlay');
  if (amask.style.display === 'block' || amask.style.display === '')
    amask.style.display = 'none';
  else
    amask.style.display = 'block'
}

<div>Since there's only 1 s orbital (holding 2 electrons).<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>s</b></span>-<b>orbital</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> electrons."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>s</b></span>-<b>orbital</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">2</span> electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since there's only 1 s orbital (holding 2 electrons).<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""></div>"
"All <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;p&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in <i>total</i> can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">6</span> <u>electrons</u>."	"All <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>p</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in <i>total</i> can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">6</span> <u>electrons</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since there can be 3 different p orbitals (each holding 2 electrons).</div><div><br></div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""></div>"
"All <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>p</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in <i>total</i> can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""6"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> <u>electrons</u>."	"All <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>p</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in <i>total</i> can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">6</span> <u>electrons</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since there can be 3 different p orbitals (each holding 2 electrons).</div><div><br></div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""></div>"
"All <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;d&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">10</span> electrons."	"All <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>d</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">10</span> electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since there can be 5 different d orbitals (each holding 2 electrons).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""><br></div></div>"
"All <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>d</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""10"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> electrons."	"All <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>d</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">10</span> electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since there can be 5 different d orbitals (each holding 2 electrons).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""><br></div></div>"
"All <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;f&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">14</span> electrons."	"All <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>f</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">14</span> electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"All <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>f</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""14"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> electrons."	"All <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>f</b></span>-<b>orbitals</b>&nbsp;in&nbsp;<i>total&nbsp;</i>can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">14</span> electrons.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since there can be 7 different f orbitals (each holding 2 electrons).<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""></div>"
"The <b style="""">f</b>-orbital has <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> <u>energy</u> than the <b style="""">s</b>-orbital."	"The <b style="""">f</b>-orbital has <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> <u>energy</u> than the <b style="""">s</b>-orbital.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Higher azimuthal (ℓ), the higher the energy.</div><div><br></div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""><br></div>"
"<i>Higher </i><b>azimuthal numbers</b> (orbital shapes) have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> <u>energies</u>."	"<i>Higher </i><b>azimuthal numbers</b> (orbital shapes) have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">higher</i></span> <u>energies</u>.

"When writing short-hand <b>electron configurations</b>, use...<div><br></div><div><b>[</b><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;noble&#x20;gas&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[element group]</span><b>]</b>3s<sup>2</sup>3p<sup>4</sup>...</div>"	"When writing short-hand <b>electron configurations</b>, use...<div><br></div><div><b>[</b><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>noble gas</u></span><b>]</b>3s<sup>2</sup>3p<sup>4</sup>...</div>

"<div>Elements can be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;identified&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>by their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>emission</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>absorption</b></span> <b>spectra</b>, since each has a unique set of orbital arrangements.</div>"	"<div>Elements can be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>identified</u></i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>by their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>emission</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>absorption</b></span> <b>spectra</b>, since each has a unique set of orbital arrangements.</div>

"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Aufbau&#x20;principle&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says that electrons fill <i>completely</i> into subshells with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>increasing energy</u></span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aufbau principle</b></span> says that electrons fill <i>completely</i> into subshells with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>increasing energy</u></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""8a8f62892997c4af566795710bc6f8ad.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aufbau principle</b></span> says that electrons fill <i>completely</i> into subshells with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;increasing&#x20;energy&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Aufbau principle</b></span> says that electrons fill <i>completely</i> into subshells with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>increasing energy</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""8a8f62892997c4af566795710bc6f8ad.png""></div>"
"<div>In electron confirgurations, the <b>n+ℓ</b><b style=""""><font color=""#222222"">&nbsp;</font>rule</b>&nbsp;can help <i>rank </i>subshells<span style=""color: rgb(34, 34, 34);""> </span>by&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;increasing&#x20;energy&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div></div>"	"<div>In electron confirgurations, the <b>n+ℓ</b><b style=""""><font color=""#222222"">&nbsp;</font>rule</b>&nbsp;can help <i>rank </i>subshells<span style=""color: rgb(34, 34, 34);""> </span>by&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>increasing energy</i></u></span>.</div><div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Use this when including <i>transition metals</i> in the configuration.</div><div><br></div><img src=""8a8f62892997c4af566795710bc6f8ad.png""></div>"
"<div>What is the <b style="""">n +&nbsp;</b><b>ℓ rule</b>&nbsp;in electron configurations?</div><div><span style=""color: rgb(34, 34, 34);""><br></span></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Use&#x20;it&#x20;to&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;compare&#x20;2&#x20;elements&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;to&#x20;find&#x20;which&#x20;subshell&#x20;&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;fills&#x20;first&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x2E;&#x20;Add&#x20;both&#x20;elements&#x27;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;b&gt;n&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x20;&#x28;period&#x20;&#x23;&#x29;&#x20;&#x2B;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;b&gt;ℓ&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x28;shape&#x29;&#x2E;&#x2E;&#x2E;&#x20;&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;smaller&#x20;number&#x20;fills&#x20;first&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div><div></div>"	"<div>What is the <b style="""">n +&nbsp;</b><b>ℓ rule</b>&nbsp;in electron configurations?</div><div><span style=""color: rgb(34, 34, 34);""><br></span></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Use it to <i><u>compare 2 elements</u></i> to find which subshell <i><u>fills first</u></i>. Add both elements'&nbsp;<b>n</b> (period #) +&nbsp;<b>ℓ </b>(shape)... <b><i>smaller number fills first</i></b>.</span><br></div><div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""68699725975ea34298c704b4dad3370c.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Hund&#x27;s&#x20;rule&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> says electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">fill into their&nbsp;<i>own</i> orbitals&nbsp;<i>before</i>&nbsp;pairing up</span>.</div><div></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hund's rule</b></span> says electrons <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">fill into their&nbsp;<i>own</i> orbitals&nbsp;<i>before</i>&nbsp;pairing up</span>.</div><div></div>

"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hund's rule</b></span> says electrons <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""fill&#x20;into&#x20;their&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;own&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;orbitals&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;before&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;pairing&#x20;up"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div><div></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Hund's rule</b></span> says electrons <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">fill into their&nbsp;<i>own</i> orbitals&nbsp;<i>before</i>&nbsp;pairing up</span>.</div><div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...based on electronic repulsion.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""7b02e314e57d057c04e555bdaddde57c.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Electron orbitals</b> that are either exactly <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;half&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> filled or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;fully&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> filled are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>most </i>stable</u></span>.</div><div></div>"	"<div><b>Electron orbitals</b> that are either exactly <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>half</i></span> filled or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>fully</i></span> filled are the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>most </i>stable</u></span>.</div><div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Hence why Cr &amp; Cu have configurations where a 4s<sup>2</sup> electron goes up&nbsp;<i>into </i>3d<sup>4</sup> to become 4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>5</sup> (more stable when it's exactly half-filled).</div><div>- A half-filled or fully-filled d orbital is more stable than a half-filled or fully-filled s orbital.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Periodic_Table.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><b>Electron orbitals</b> that are either exactly <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>half</i></span> filled or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>fully</i></span> filled are the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;most&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;stable&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div><div></div>"	"<div><b>Electron orbitals</b> that are either exactly <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>half</i></span> filled or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>fully</i></span> filled are the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>most </i>stable</u></span>.</div><div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Hence why Cr &amp; Cu have configurations where a 4s<sup>2</sup> electron goes up&nbsp;<i>into </i>3d<sup>4</sup> to become 4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>5</sup> (more stable when it's exactly half-filled).</div><div>- A half-filled or fully-filled d orbital is more stable than a half-filled or fully-filled s orbital.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Periodic_Table.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Elements in the same group as <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Cr&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Cu&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <i><u>promote</u></i> an electron from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">4s<sup style="""">2</sup></span> orbital to <i>fill </i>either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">5</sup></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">10</sup></span>.</div><div></div>"	"<div>Elements in the same group as <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cr</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cu</b></span> <i><u>promote</u></i> an electron from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">4s<sup style="""">2</sup></span> orbital to <i>fill </i>either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">5</sup></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">10</sup></span>.</div><div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>4s<sup>2</sup>3d<sup>4</sup> → 4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>5</sup></div><div>- 3d<sup>5</sup> because it's exactly half filled (more stable) or 3d<sup>10</sup> because it's fully filled (more stable).</div><div>- Notice how Cr &amp; Cu are in the d-groups that contain either 3d<sup>4</sup> or 3d<sup>9</sup>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Periodic_Table.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Elements in the same group as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cr</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cu</b></span> <i><u>promote</u></i> an electron from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""4s&lt;sup&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> orbital to <i>fill </i>either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">5</sup></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">10</sup></span>.</div><div></div>"	"<div>Elements in the same group as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cr</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cu</b></span> <i><u>promote</u></i> an electron from the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">4s<sup style="""">2</sup></span> orbital to <i>fill </i>either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">5</sup></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">10</sup></span>.</div><div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>4s<sup>2</sup>3d<sup>4</sup> → 4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>5</sup></div><div>- 3d<sup>5</sup> because it's exactly half filled (more stable) or 3d<sup>10</sup> because it's fully filled (more stable).</div><div>- Notice how Cr &amp; Cu are in the d-groups that contain either 3d<sup>4</sup> or 3d<sup>9</sup>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Periodic_Table.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Elements in the same group as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cr</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cu</b></span> <i><u>promote</u></i> an electron from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">4s<sup style="""">2</sup></span> orbital to <i>fill </i>either <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3d&lt;sup&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;5&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3d&lt;sup&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;10&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>.</div><div></div>"	"<div>Elements in the same group as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cr</b></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Cu</b></span> <i><u>promote</u></i> an electron from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">4s<sup style="""">2</sup></span> orbital to <i>fill </i>either <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">5</sup></span> or <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">3d<sup style="""">10</sup></span>.</div><div></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>4s<sup>2</sup>3d<sup>4</sup> → 4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>5</sup></div><div>- 3d<sup>5</sup> because it's exactly half filled (more stable) or 3d<sup>10</sup> because it's fully filled (more stable).</div><div>- Notice how Cr &amp; Cu are in the d-groups that contain either 3d<sup>4</sup> or 3d<sup>9</sup>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Periodic_Table.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Paramagnetic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> materials have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>unpaired </i>electrons</u></span> that can orient their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spins</i></span> parallel to magnetic fields.</div><div></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Paramagnetic</b></span> materials have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>unpaired </i>electrons</u></span> that can orient their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spins</i></span> parallel to magnetic fields.</div><div></div>

"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Diamagnetic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> materials have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>fully<i> </i>paired electrons</u></span> that experience <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>repulsions</i></span> from magnetic fields - orienting their spins in anti-parallel directions.</div><div></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Diamagnetic</b></span> materials have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>fully<i> </i>paired electrons</u></span> that experience <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>repulsions</i></span> from magnetic fields - orienting their spins in anti-parallel directions.</div><div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Since their electrons are fully paired, they <i>can't </i>orient them in the same direction (magnetism).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""30b67a94f86ef15daaa59481a82af28b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-90fff3e9909b1446271768aec5572d428404edac.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"What's the <i>difference </i>between an atom's <b>shell</b> &amp; <b>subshell</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Shell</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""describes&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;energy&#x20;levels&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x28;n&#x3D;1&#x2C;&#x20;n&#x3D;2&#x2C;&#x20;etc&#x2E;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div><b>Subshell</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""describes&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;orbital&#x20;shapes&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;within&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;shells&#x20;&#x28;s&#x2C;&#x20;p&#x2C;&#x20;d&#x2C;&#x20;f&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"What's the <i>difference </i>between an atom's <b>shell</b> &amp; <b>subshell</b>?<div><br></div><div><b>Shell</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">describes <u><i>energy levels</i></u> (n=1, n=2, etc.)</span></div><div><b>Subshell</b>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">describes <u><i>orbital shapes</i></u> <i>within</i> shells (s, p, d, f)</span></div>

"A <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;strong&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[strong/weak]</span>&nbsp;<b>effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b>&nbsp;makes the atomic radius <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">smaller</i></span>."	"A <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">strong</i></span>&nbsp;<b>effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b>&nbsp;makes the atomic radius <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">smaller</i></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b> is the strength of the ""pull"" an atom's protons have on its valence electrons.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""5afa5f7b99002dec005b68ac9801e03e.png""><br></div></div>"
"A <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">strong</i></span>&nbsp;<b>effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b>&nbsp;makes the atomic radius <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;smaller&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[larger/smaller]</span>."	"A <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">strong</i></span>&nbsp;<b>effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b>&nbsp;makes the atomic radius <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">smaller</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b> is the strength of the ""pull"" an atom's protons have on its valence electrons.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""5afa5f7b99002dec005b68ac9801e03e.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;decreased&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increased/decreased]</span> by other <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>non-valence electrons</u></span> acting as <i>shields</i>."	"<b>Effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">decreased</i></span> by other <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>non-valence electrons</u></span> acting as <i>shields</i>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""effective-nuclear.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""934bb40e70727502ceab808ce24d817b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">decreased</i></span> by other <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;non&#x2D;valence&#x20;electrons&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> acting as <i>shields</i>."	"<b>Effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">decreased</i></span> by other <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>non-valence electrons</u></span> acting as <i>shields</i>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""effective-nuclear.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""934bb40e70727502ceab808ce24d817b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Effective nucelar charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;stays&amp;nbsp&#x3B;constant&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> as you move from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">top</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">bottom</span>."	"<b>Effective nucelar charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>stays&nbsp;constant</i></u></span> as you move from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">top</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">bottom</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Constant since the number of protons increases (stronger attraction) but the number of shielding electrons also increases - cancelling each other out.</div><div>- This is the strength positively charged nuclei have on their valence electrons.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""5afa5f7b99002dec005b68ac9801e03e.png""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Effective nucelar charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>stays&nbsp;constant</i></u></span> as you move from <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""top"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""bottom"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Effective nucelar charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>stays&nbsp;constant</i></u></span> as you move from <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">top</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">bottom</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Constant since the number of protons increases (stronger attraction) but the number of shielding electrons also increases - cancelling each other out.</div><div>- This is the strength positively charged nuclei have on their valence electrons.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""5afa5f7b99002dec005b68ac9801e03e.png""><br></div></div>"
"An atom's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;radius&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is calculated as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">half&nbsp;</i>the distance</span> between <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>two atoms</u>&nbsp;in contact</span>."	"An atom's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>radius</b></span> is calculated as <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">half&nbsp;</i>the distance</span> between <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>two atoms</u>&nbsp;in contact</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...can't calculate with just 1 atom since the electrons are constantly moving.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""6-post-03c.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"An atom's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>radius</b></span> is calculated as <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;half&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;the&#x20;distance"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> between <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;two&#x20;atoms&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;in&#x20;contact"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"An atom's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>radius</b></span> is calculated as <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">half&nbsp;</i>the distance</span> between <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>two atoms</u>&nbsp;in contact</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Even if the standard state for the element is a liquid or solid, the definition still stands as beng in the gaseous state.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ionization energy</b></span> is the amount of energy required to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;remove&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[add/remove]</span> an electron from an atom in its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;gaseous&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[phase]</span> state."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ionization energy</b></span> is the amount of energy required to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">remove</i></span> an electron from an atom in its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>gaseous</u></span> state.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Even if the standard state for the element is a liquid or solid, the definition still stands as beng in the gaseous state.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div>"
"<b>Ionization energy</b>&nbsp;is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""endo"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>thermic (for the removal of an electron)."	"<b>Ionization energy</b>&nbsp;is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">endo</span>thermic (for the removal of an electron).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Requires <i>heat </i>to remove the electron.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div>"
"<b>Ionization energy</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreases]</span> from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">left</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">right</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">bottom</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">top</span>."	"<b>Ionization energy</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">increases</i></span> from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">left</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">right</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">bottom</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">top</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because it's <i>harder</i> to remove electrons from the ones closest to finally achieving their octet.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div>"
"<b>Ionization energy</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">increases</i></span> from <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""left"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""right"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">bottom</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">top</span>."	"<b>Ionization energy</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">increases</i></span> from <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">left</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">right</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">bottom</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">top</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because it's <i>harder</i> to remove electrons from the ones closest to finally achieving their octet.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>higher</i></u></span><i>&nbsp;</i>the atom's Z<sub>eff</sub>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> its <b>ionization</b>&nbsp;<b>energy</b>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>higher</i></u></span><i>&nbsp;</i>the atom's Z<sub>eff</sub>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i><u>higher</u></i></span> its <b>ionization</b>&nbsp;<b>energy</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>I.e. harder it is to remove an electron.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;first&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;ionization&#x20;energy"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the energy required to <i>remove </i>the 1<sup>st</sup> electron from an atom."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>first </i>ionization energy</span> is the energy required to <i>remove </i>the 1<sup>st</sup> electron from an atom.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Releases heat since it's <u>so</u> energetically favorable.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""electron_affinity_city_w.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Electron&#x20;affinity&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the amount of energy <i>released</i> when atoms <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u><i>gain</i></u></span> electrons." <div>Electronegative atoms <u><i>do not</i></u> want to lose electrons.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9c5f4a23a3e0cbfb933a0e5156bffe15.png""><br></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""9c5f4a23a3e0cbfb933a0e5156bffe15.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Noble&#x20;gases&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[elements]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">are not</i></span>&nbsp;<b>electronegative</b>." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""8b6787e10cfacd731b296364ab0917bb.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>For <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;non&#x2D;metals&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[metals/non-metals]</span>, losing electrons is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>hard</i></u></span>.<br></div>"	"<div>For <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>non-metals</b></span>, losing electrons is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>hard</i></u></span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""bf0de775f281e51a5ce82a10bf620324.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[higher/lower]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>the atom's <b>Z<sub>eff</sub></b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> its <u>electron affinity</u>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">higher</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>the atom's <b>Z<sub>eff</sub></b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> its <u>electron affinity</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...releases <i>a lot</i> of heat when it gets what it wants (another electron).</div><div><br></div><div><div><b>Metals</b>: negative electron affinity (want to lose electrons)</div><div><b>Non-metals</b>: positive electron affinity (want to gain electrons)</div><div><b>Halogens</b>: zero electron affinity</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""electron_affinity_city_w.png""></div></div></div>"
"<b>Ionization energy</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">increases</i></span> from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">left</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">right</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""bottom"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""top"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Ionization energy</b>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">increases</i></span> from <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">left</span> to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">right</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">bottom</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">top</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because it's <i>harder</i> to remove electrons from the ones closest to finally achieving their octet.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div>"
"The ionic complexes of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>transition metals</b></span> change <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""colors"" data-ordinal=""4"">[...]</span> when their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>d-orbital electrons</u></span> <i>transition </i>between subshells."	"The ionic complexes of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>transition metals</b></span> change <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""4"">colors</span> when their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>d-orbital electrons</u></span> <i>transition </i>between subshells.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Not all d-orbitals in an ionic complex have the same energy... they transition back and forth.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a-che-tran_metal-dia03.gif""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8b6787e10cfacd731b296364ab0917bb.png""><br></div></div>"
"A <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[high/low]</span> <b>ionization energy</b> means it's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>hard</i></u></span> to <i>pull off</i> an electron from an atom."	"A <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>high</i></u></span> <b>ionization energy</b> means it's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>hard</i></u></span> to <i>pull off</i> an electron from an atom.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"A <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>high</i></u></span> <b>ionization energy</b> means it's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;hard&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[easy/hard]</span> to <i>pull off</i> an electron from an atom."	"A <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>high</i></u></span> <b>ionization energy</b> means it's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>hard</i></u></span> to <i>pull off</i> an electron from an atom.

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Lithium</b></span> is the only group 1 metal that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>violates</i></span> the octet rule.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Lithium</b></span> is the only group 1 metal that <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;violates&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> the octet rule."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Lithium</b></span> is the only group 1 metal that <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>violates</i></span> the octet rule.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Loses 1 electron to become more stable... only has 2 in its inner shell since it happens to be the first element in the group.</div><div><br></div><img src=""0df777c3d6a151e152f82825802733ab.png""><br></div>"
"<i>Why </i>does <b>Lithium</b> violate the octet rule?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Has&#x20;3&#x20;total&#x20;electrons&#x2E;&#x20;Since&#x20;it&#x20;loses&#x20;1&#x20;to&#x20;become&#x20;more&#x20;stable&#x20;&#x28;Li&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x29;&#x2C;&#x20;ends&#x20;up&#x20;resembling&#x20;Helium&#x20;&#x28;only&#x20;2&#x20;electrons&#x20;left&#x29;&#x2E;&#x20;This&#x20;occurs&#x20;since&#x20;its&#x20;the&#x20;first&#x20;element&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;group&#x20;1&#x20;metals&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<i>Why </i>does <b>Lithium</b> violate the octet rule?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Has 3 total electrons. Since it loses 1 to become more stable (Li<sup>+</sup>), ends up resembling Helium (only 2 electrons left). This occurs since its the first element of the group 1 metals.</span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Ionic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ionic/covalent]</span> bonds have <u><i>higher</i></u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">melting</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">boiling</span> points than <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;covalent&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ionic/covalent]</span>&nbsp;bonds."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ionic</b></span> bonds have <u><i>higher</i></u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">melting</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">boiling</span> points than <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>covalent</b></span>&nbsp;bonds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

...due to their extreme attraction to one another.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ionic</b></span> bonds have <u><i>higher</i></u> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""melting"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""boiling"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> points than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>covalent</b></span>&nbsp;bonds."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Ionic</b></span> bonds have <u><i>higher</i></u> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">melting</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">boiling</span> points than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>covalent</b></span>&nbsp;bonds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

...due to their extreme attraction to one another.</div>"
"Molecules with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ionic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>bonds</b> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>good</i></u></span> conductors of electricity."	"Molecules with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ionic</b></span> <b>bonds</b> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>good</i></u></span> conductors of electricity.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...are charged when they ionize</div><div><br></div>- NaCl</div>"
"Molecules with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ionic</b></span> <b>bonds</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;good&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[good/bad]</span> conductors of electricity."	"Molecules with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ionic</b></span> <b>bonds</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>good</i></u></span> conductors of electricity.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...are charged when they ionize</div><div><br></div>- NaCl</div>"
"<i>Why </i>are molecules with <b>covalent bonds</b> <i><u>bad</u></i> conductors of electricity?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Covalent&#x20;molecules&#x20;split&#x20;&quot;equally&quot;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;instead&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;of&#x20;forming&#x20;charged&#x20;ions&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<i>Why </i>are molecules with <b>covalent bonds</b> <i><u>bad</u></i> conductors of electricity?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Covalent molecules split ""equally"" <i>instead </i>of forming charged ions.</span></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Bond&#x20;energy&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the <i>amount </i>of energy required to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>break </i>a bond</span> in a molecule."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Bond energy</b></span> is the <i>amount </i>of energy required to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>break </i>a bond</span> in a molecule.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Also called dissociation energy</div>- <b>Triple </b>bonds have <u>higher</u> bond energies than double or single bonds.<div><br></div><div><img src=""45c0d376b9ba497979959810ed71efbe.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Bond energy</b></span> is the <i>amount </i>of energy required to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;break&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;a&#x20;bond"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> in a molecule."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Bond energy</b></span> is the <i>amount </i>of energy required to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>break </i>a bond</span> in a molecule.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Also called dissociation energy</div>- <b>Triple </b>bonds have <u>higher</u> bond energies than double or single bonds.<div><br></div><div><img src=""45c0d376b9ba497979959810ed71efbe.png""><br></div></div>"
"<u>Triple</u> bonds have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;greater&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[greater/less]</span> <b style="""">bond energies</b> than <u>double</u> bonds."	"<u>Triple</u> bonds have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">greater</i></span> <b style="""">bond energies</b> than <u>double</u> bonds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

...due to its additional electron.<div>- Takes <u>more energy</u> to break a bond apart in a <b>triple </b>bond.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""45c0d376b9ba497979959810ed71efbe.png""><br></div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Beginning with <b>NOF</b>, these form the <b>number 7</b> on the periodic table (excluding H).</div><div><br></div><img src=""rjiSbp7euqxFHpIOWM1Tz1nGDYyMMbG4mdn33MrU_nf_OKkXcVMuS1i9MeSnM3-DKSgpqQGvSsHV6H6u.jpg""></div>"
"<div>The <b><u>ionic</u></b> radii of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""metals"" data-ordinal=""1"">[metals/non-metals]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>small</i></u></span>.<br></div>"	"<div>The <b><u>ionic</u></b> radii of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">metals</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>small</i></u></span>.<br></div>

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Tells you how many electrons can be in each subshell (n=1, n=2, n=3, etc.)</div><div><br></div>- n = principal quantum number.</div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""gas"" data-ordinal=""1"">[solid/liquid/gas]</span> phase has the <i>highest </i>degree of <u>kinetic enegry</u>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">gas</span> phase has the <i>highest </i>degree of <u>kinetic enegry</u>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>Real</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>gases <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;do&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[do/do not]</span> have intermolecular forces &amp; volumes." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""68mG7d1JvjhhPv8sMLG6kD6vq49xhDMFtulIWJUqMn1SBXsMbtBu6AwSmYGF47SpPjooz6QPCyWuYTqcglplwRKpYQ.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;Real&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[idea/real]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>gases <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>do</i></u></span> have intermolecular forces &amp; volumes." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""68mG7d1JvjhhPv8sMLG6kD6vq49xhDMFtulIWJUqMn1SBXsMbtBu6AwSmYGF47SpPjooz6QPCyWuYTqcglplwRKpYQ.png""></div>"
"<b>Real&nbsp;gases</b> obey the same laws as <u>ideal gases</u> <i>only </i>when they <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;are&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[are/are not]</span> interacting with each other."	"<b>Real&nbsp;gases</b> obey the same laws as <u>ideal gases</u> <i>only </i>when they <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>are not</i></span> interacting with each other.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""68mG7d1JvjhhPv8sMLG6kD6vq49xhDMFtulIWJUqMn1SBXsMbtBu6AwSmYGF47SpPjooz6QPCyWuYTqcglplwRKpYQ.png""></div>"
"Real gases&nbsp;<i>behave </i>like&nbsp;<b>ideal gases</b> when <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;pressure&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;temperature&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span>."	"Real gases&nbsp;<i>behave </i>like&nbsp;<b>ideal gases</b> when <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>pressure</u></span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>temperature</u></span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Pressure</b>: molecules would be more ""spread out.""</div><div><b>Temperature</b>: gives them enough kinetic energy to avoid ""sticking"" when they collide and instead ""bounce away"" (<u>minimizing</u> intermolecular forces).</div><div><u><br></u></div><div><u>Minimizes</u> the amount of ""interacting"" they do. Real gases behave like ideal gases when they're <u>not</u> in contact with each other.</div><div><br></div><img src=""68mG7d1JvjhhPv8sMLG6kD6vq49xhDMFtulIWJUqMn1SBXsMbtBu6AwSmYGF47SpPjooz6QPCyWuYTqcglplwRKpYQ.png""></div>"
"Real gases&nbsp;<i>behave </i>like&nbsp;<b>ideal gases</b> when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>pressure</u></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[high/low]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>temperature</u></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[high/low]</span>."	"Real gases&nbsp;<i>behave </i>like&nbsp;<b>ideal gases</b> when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>pressure</u></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>temperature</u></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Pressure</b>: molecules would be more ""spread out.""</div><div><b>Temperature</b>: gives them enough kinetic energy to avoid ""sticking"" when they collide and instead ""bounce away"" (<u>minimizing</u> intermolecular forces).</div><div><u><br></u></div><div><u>Minimizes</u> the amount of ""interacting"" they do. Real gases behave like ideal gases when they're <u>not</u> in contact with each other.</div><div><br></div><img src=""68mG7d1JvjhhPv8sMLG6kD6vq49xhDMFtulIWJUqMn1SBXsMbtBu6AwSmYGF47SpPjooz6QPCyWuYTqcglplwRKpYQ.png""></div>"
"Do <b>ideal gases</b> truly exist?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;u&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;only&#x20;real&#x20;gases&#x20;exist&#x2E;&#x20;Real&#x20;gases&#x20;behave&#x20;similarly&#x20;to&#x20;ideal&#x20;gases&#x20;only&#x20;when&#x20;they&#x27;re&#x20;&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;in&#x20;contact&#x20;with&#x20;each&#x20;other&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Do <b>ideal gases</b> truly exist?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><u>No</u></b>, only real gases exist. Real gases behave similarly to ideal gases only when they're <u><i>not</i></u> in contact with each other.</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""68mG7d1JvjhhPv8sMLG6kD6vq49xhDMFtulIWJUqMn1SBXsMbtBu6AwSmYGF47SpPjooz6QPCyWuYTqcglplwRKpYQ.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Gas&#x20;pressure&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the amount of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>force per area</u></i></span> gas molecules exert <i>on </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>container walls</u></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gas pressure</b></span> is the amount of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>force per area</u></i></span> gas molecules exert <i>on </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>container walls</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Depends both on <b><u>collision force</u></b> &amp; <b><u>collision frequency</u></b>. Don't confuse ""force per area"" with collision force...</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""l0H9eIEepvPkZ3ZwaFl036d4FjxXlJJE1LFLJg6C9ajwOFVEMfhh_cUCHm6Sd-Jlq3m0D_50J2tvXxS1CRqT5PpSrHtodcvI4GNaD6PzUxNS2lz6DuU9NI""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""75b8af2e59259269d66c51fcfbfdb28a.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gas pressure</b></span> is the amount of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;&lt;u&gt;force&#x20;per&#x20;area&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> gas molecules exert <i>on </i>the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;container&#x20;walls&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Gas pressure</b></span> is the amount of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i><u>force per area</u></i></span> gas molecules exert <i>on </i>the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>container walls</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Depends both on <b><u>collision force</u></b> &amp; <b><u>collision frequency</u></b>. Don't confuse ""force per area"" with collision force...</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""l0H9eIEepvPkZ3ZwaFl036d4FjxXlJJE1LFLJg6C9ajwOFVEMfhh_cUCHm6Sd-Jlq3m0D_50J2tvXxS1CRqT5PpSrHtodcvI4GNaD6PzUxNS2lz6DuU9NI""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""75b8af2e59259269d66c51fcfbfdb28a.png""><br></div></div>"
"For gases, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[more/less]</span><i>&nbsp;</i><u>collisions</u> with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;greater&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[greater/weaker]</span><i>&nbsp;</i><u>force</u> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>increases</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pressure</b></span>." <div>Increases each molecule's kinetic energy, so they have <u>greater<b> momentum</b></u>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""Real+Gas+Effect+of+intermolecular+forces+on+the+pressure+exerted+by+a+gas..jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""75b8af2e59259269d66c51fcfbfdb28a.png""><br></div></div>"

"According to the <u>kinetic theory of gases</u>, the&nbsp;<i>collisions </i>of gas molecules&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""perfectly&#x20;elastic"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, meaning <i>no</i> energy is lost (heat)."	"According to the <u>kinetic theory of gases</u>, the&nbsp;<i>collisions </i>of gas molecules&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">perfectly elastic</span>, meaning <i>no</i> energy is lost (heat).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b><u>Momentum</u></b> is conserved <b><u>100%</u></b> (hence perfectly elastic).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-50740ee807aec7683566a2c68609238b0d6a3229.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-39e4e0f6e223da8f793797e6c999dbc52bbbe6ab.jpg""><br></div>"
"According to the <u>kinetic theory of gases</u>, gas molecules move in <i>perfectly</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""straight&#x20;lines"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> and <i>only</i> change directions when they <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""collide"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"According to the <u>kinetic theory of gases</u>, gas molecules move in <i>perfectly</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">straight lines</span> and <i>only</i> change directions when they <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">collide</span>.

"According to the <u>kinetic theory of gases</u>, gas molecules are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>always</i></span> moving.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-39e4e0f6e223da8f793797e6c999dbc52bbbe6ab.jpg""></div>"
"According to the <u>kinetic theory of gases</u>, gas molecules <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;do&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[do/do not]</span> have <b>intermolecular forces</b>."	"According to the <u>kinetic theory of gases</u>, gas molecules <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>do not</i></u></span> have <b>intermolecular forces</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This theory pretends like all gases are <b><u>ideal gases</u></b>.</div><div><b><u>Real gases</u></b> behave like ideal gases when they're <u>not</u> in contact. When they collide, they <u>do</u> exhibit intermolecular forces.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-39e4e0f6e223da8f793797e6c999dbc52bbbe6ab.jpg""><br></div>"
"Gas particles with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;greater&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[greater/lower]</span> masses<b> </b><i>collide </i>with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">greater</i></span> force<b>&nbsp;</b>against container walls."	"Gas particles with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">greater</i></span> masses<b> </b><i>collide </i>with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">greater</i></span> force<b>&nbsp;</b>against container walls.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>They have higher masses, so they have more <b><u>momentum</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
"Gas particles with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">greater</i></span> masses<b> </b><i>collide </i>with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;greater&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[greater/lower]</span> force<b>&nbsp;</b>against container walls."	"Gas particles with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">greater</i></span> masses<b> </b><i>collide </i>with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i style="""">greater</i></span> force<b>&nbsp;</b>against container walls.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>They have higher masses, so they have more <b><u>momentum</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
"Gas particles with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;greater&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[greater/lower]</span> <b>masses </b>move <i>slightly </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>slower</i></span>."	"Gas particles with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">greater</i></span> <b>masses </b>move <i>slightly </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>slower</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since they move only slightly slower but have greater masses, they have <b><u>more momentum</u></b> and exert <b><u>greater forces</u></b> against the container walls.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
"Gas particles with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">greater</i></span> <b>masses </b>move <i>slightly </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;slower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[faster/slower]</span>."	"Gas particles with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i style="""">greater</i></span> <b>masses </b>move <i>slightly </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>slower</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Since they move only slightly slower but have greater masses, they have <b><u>more momentum</u></b> and exert <b><u>greater forces</u></b> against the container walls.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;Lighter&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[heavier/lighter]</span> gas molecules have <i>less</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>momentum</b></span> and exert <i>less </i>force against the container walls."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>Lighter</i></u></span> gas molecules have <i>less</i> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>momentum</b></span> and exert <i>less </i>force against the container walls.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Heavier &amp; lighter gas molecules still result in the <b><u>same gas pressure</u></b> against the walls, since pressure depends both on <b><u>collision force</u></b> &amp; <b><u>collision frequency</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>Lighter</i></u></span> gas molecules have <i>less</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;momentum&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> and exert <i>less </i>force against the container walls."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>Lighter</i></u></span> gas molecules have <i>less</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>momentum</b></span> and exert <i>less </i>force against the container walls.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Heavier &amp; lighter gas molecules still result in the <b><u>same gas pressure</u></b> against the walls, since pressure depends both on <b><u>collision force</u></b> &amp; <b><u>collision frequency</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
