"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Heavier</span> gas molecules have <i>more</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">momentum</b></span> and exert <i>more</i>&nbsp;force against the container walls.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Lighter &amp; heavier gas molecules both exert the <u>same gas pressure</u> against the walls, since pressure relies on both collision force &amp; collision frequency</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Heavier</span> gas molecules have <i>more</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;&quot;&gt;momentum&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> and exert <i>more</i>&nbsp;force against the container walls."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Heavier</span> gas molecules have <i>more</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b style="""">momentum</b></span> and exert <i>more</i>&nbsp;force against the container walls.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Lighter &amp; heavier gas molecules both exert the <u>same gas pressure</u> against the walls, since pressure relies on both collision force &amp; collision frequency</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
"Gas molecules of <i>different</i> masses exert <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;the&#x20;same&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[the same/different]</span> <b>pressure </b>against the container walls."	"Gas molecules of <i>different</i> masses exert <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>the same</u></span> <b>pressure </b>against the container walls.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Gas pressure depends on both <b><u>collision force</u></b> &amp; <b><u>collision frequency</u></b>. Heavier molecules have greater force but less collisions, and ligher molecules have less force but more collisions... balanced out.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""907a47f9496d965c22ffbf662dd4faff.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Gas pressure</b></span> depends on both the gas molecules' collision <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;force&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; collision <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;frequency&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Gas pressure</b></span> depends on both the gas molecules' collision <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>force</i></span> &amp; collision <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>frequency</i></span>.

"Do <i>all </i><b>gas molecules</b> in a container have the <i>exact same</i> <u>kinetic energy</u>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;u&gt;No&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&#x2C;&#x20;each&#x20;might&#x20;have&#x20;slightly&#x20;different&#x20;kinetic&#x20;energies&#x2C;&#x20;but&#x20;the&#x20;average&#x20;is&#x20;assumed&#x20;to&#x20;be&#x20;the&#x20;same&#x20;&#x28;by&#x20;definition&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;word&#x20;temperature&#x29;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Do <i>all </i><b>gas molecules</b> in a container have the <i>exact same</i> <u>kinetic energy</u>?<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><u>No</u></b>, each might have slightly different kinetic energies, but the average is assumed to be the same (by definition of the word temperature).</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Temperature is a measure of the <u>average</u> kinetic energy.</div><div><br></div><img src=""97efd705ce50d296ed32bb68cad3f2fb.png""></div>"
"The <b>kinetic theory of gases</b> describes <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ideal"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ideal/real]</span> gases."	"The <b>kinetic theory of gases</b> describes <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ideal</span> gases.

"In gases, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> <u>temperatures</u>&nbsp;result in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> <u>volumes</u>." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b><u>Charle's law</u></b></div><div>- ↑temperature causes gas molecules to bounce around more and increase the ""size"" of the gas (↑volume). Think of <b><u>thermometers</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><div><img src=""fcc31c63994906d0c57a292eafc989c6.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""9a8d34360113b555042092dc38e5dd40.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a52a9e49e53dd2a5520a1aeb2e8606147ad76db3.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"In gases, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> <u>temperatures</u>&nbsp;result in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> <u>volumes</u>.
<hr>

Think of <b><u>thermometers</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><div><img src=""fcc31c63994906d0c57a292eafc989c6.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""9a8d34360113b555042092dc38e5dd40.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a52a9e49e53dd2a5520a1aeb2e8606147ad76db3.jpg""><br></div></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>More collisions to cause the gas to expand (↑volume).</div><div><br></div><img src=""08beb446d46233a83f3d5c0be17c7bd6.png""></div>"
"Gases behave <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> like <u>ideal gases</u> when <b>volume</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increases/decreased]</span>."	"Gases behave <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> like <u>ideal gases</u> when <b>volume</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Gives them <u>more room to spread out</u>... less likely to collide.</div>"
"Gases behave <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[more/less]</span> like <u>ideal gases</u> when <b>volume</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span>."	"Gases behave <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>more</i></span> like <u>ideal gases</u> when <b>volume</b> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increases</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Gives them <u>more room to spread out</u>... less likely to collide.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>STP conditions</b></span>&nbsp;used in the <u>ideal gas law</u>:<div><br></div><div>Pressure = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x20;atm"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>Temperature = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""273&#x20;K&#x20;&#x28;0&#xB0;C&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>STP conditions</b></span>&nbsp;used in the <u>ideal gas law</u>:<div><br></div><div>Pressure = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 atm</span></div><div>Temperature = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">273 K (0°C)</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

These STP conditions are standardized because the system's final equilibrium will be different depending on what the pressure or temperature is.</div>"
"The <b><u>ideal gas law</u></b> equation (<b>PV=nRT</b>) describes both the <u style="""">properties</u> of a <i>single </i>gas &amp; its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;before&#x2D;and&#x2D;after&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> case when variables get changed."	"The <b><u>ideal gas law</u></b> equation (<b>PV=nRT</b>) describes both the <u style="""">properties</u> of a <i>single </i>gas &amp; its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>before-and-after</u></span> case when variables get changed.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""bc995caf5fca42d5476d23de51be46fe.png""><b><u><br></u></b></div><b><u><div><b><u><br></u></b></div>Properties of the <i>single </i>gas</u></b>...<div>- ""What's the volume of a gas with __ pressure at __ temperature?"" (use PV = nRT).<br></div><div><br></div><div><b><u><i>Before-and-after</i> case</u></b>...</div><div>- ""If a gas occupies __ mL at STP, what's its volume when temperature is <u>changed</u> to __?"" (use above image).</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Real</span> gases exert <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> <b>pressures </b>on the container's walls&nbsp;than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ideal</span> gases."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Real</span> gases exert <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>lower</i></u></span> <b>pressures </b>on the container's walls&nbsp;than <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ideal</span> gases.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Intermolecular forces</b> between real gases will cause them to be <u>attracted more</u> to each other and <u>collide less</u> with the wall.<div><br></div><div><img src=""b3c4aea491e23b2221ce7e4dc88018a6.png""><br><div><br></div><div><img src=""68mG7d1JvjhhPv8sMLG6kD6vq49xhDMFtulIWJUqMn1SBXsMbtBu6AwSmYGF47SpPjooz6QPCyWuYTqcglplwRKpYQ.png""><br></div></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Real"" data-ordinal=""2"">[real/ideal]</span> gases exert <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>lower</i></u></span> <b>pressures </b>on the container's walls&nbsp;than <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ideal"" data-ordinal=""2"">[real/ideal]</span> gases."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Real</span> gases exert <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>lower</i></u></span> <b>pressures </b>on the container's walls&nbsp;than <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">ideal</span> gases.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>Equation that relates <u>ideal</u>&nbsp;gas <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>pressure</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b><i>vs.</i> <u>real</u>&nbsp;gas&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>pressure</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><div>'<b>a</b>' is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>attraction</i></span> coefficient between the gas molecules (negative when particles repel).</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;b3c4aea491e23b2221ce7e4dc88018a6&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation that relates <u>ideal</u>&nbsp;gas <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>pressure</b></span><b>&nbsp;</b><i>vs.</i> <u>real</u>&nbsp;gas&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>pressure</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><div>'<b>a</b>' is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>attraction</i></span> coefficient between the gas molecules (negative when particles repel).</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><img src=""b3c4aea491e23b2221ce7e4dc88018a6.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"When the <b>pressure </b>of a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ideal</span> gas is <i>halved</i>, its <b>volume </b>will <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;double&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"When the <b>pressure </b>of a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">ideal</span> gas is <i>halved</i>, its <b>volume </b>will <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>double</u></span>.

"When the <b>pressure </b>of a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ideal"" data-ordinal=""2"">[real/ideal]</span> gas is <i>halved</i>, its <b>volume </b>will <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>double</u></span>."	"When the <b>pressure </b>of a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">ideal</span> gas is <i>halved</i>, its <b>volume </b>will <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>double</u></span>.

<img src=""699c01e9dc507b4979fb5bdd8c44a397.png""></div>"
"When the&nbsp;<b>pressure&nbsp;</b>of a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">real</span> gas is&nbsp;<i>halved</i>, its&nbsp;<b>volume </b>will&nbsp;be&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""slightly&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;less&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;than&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;double&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"When the&nbsp;<b>pressure&nbsp;</b>of a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">real</span> gas is&nbsp;<i>halved</i>, its&nbsp;<b>volume </b>will&nbsp;be&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">slightly&nbsp;<i>less</i>&nbsp;than&nbsp;<u>double</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Slightly less because the volume of the gas molecules are still the same size. V<sub>gas</sub> = V<sub>container</sub> - V<sub>gas mol.</sub>. Real gas molecules have volumes, whereas ideal gas molecules do not.</div><div><br></div><img src=""699c01e9dc507b4979fb5bdd8c44a397.png""></div>"
"When the&nbsp;<b>pressure&nbsp;</b>of a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""real"" data-ordinal=""2"">[real/ideal]</span> gas is&nbsp;<i>halved</i>, its&nbsp;<b>volume </b>will&nbsp;be&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">slightly&nbsp;<i>less</i>&nbsp;than&nbsp;<u>double</u></span>."	"When the&nbsp;<b>pressure&nbsp;</b>of a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">real</span> gas is&nbsp;<i>halved</i>, its&nbsp;<b>volume </b>will&nbsp;be&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">slightly&nbsp;<i>less</i>&nbsp;than&nbsp;<u>double</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Slightly less because the volume of the gas molecules are still the same size. V<sub>gas</sub> = V<sub>container</sub> - V<sub>gas mol.</sub>. Real gas molecules have volumes, whereas ideal gas molecules do not.</div><div><br></div><img src=""699c01e9dc507b4979fb5bdd8c44a397.png""></div>"
"<div>Use <i>this </i>form of the <b><u>ideal gas law</u></b> when comparing the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;before&#x2D;and&#x2D;after&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> case relationship of a <i>single </i>gas:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""bc995caf5fca42d5476d23de51be46fe.png""></span><br>"	"<div>Use <i>this </i>form of the <b><u>ideal gas law</u></b> when comparing the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>before-and-after</i></span> case relationship of a <i>single </i>gas:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""bc995caf5fca42d5476d23de51be46fe.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

In both before &amp; after cases, R is constant. Therefore, setting both expressions in terms of R allows you to set them equal to each other.</div>"
"<div>Use <i>this </i>form of the <b><u>ideal gas law</u></b> when comparing the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>before-and-after</i></span> case relationship of a <i>single </i>gas:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;bc995caf5fca42d5476d23de51be46fe&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Use <i>this </i>form of the <b><u>ideal gas law</u></b> when comparing the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>before-and-after</i></span> case relationship of a <i>single </i>gas:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""bc995caf5fca42d5476d23de51be46fe.png""></span><br>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

In both before &amp; after cases, R is constant. Therefore, setting both expressions in terms of R allows you to set them equal to each other.</div>"

"A compound's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;empirical&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[molecular/empirical]</span> <b>formula </b>is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>most simplified</i>&nbsp;ratio</span> between its elements."	"A compound's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>empirical</b></span> <b>formula </b>is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>most simplified</i>&nbsp;ratio</span> between its elements.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""Most simplified"" meaning it cannot be simplified any further (like C<sub>3</sub>H<sub>8</sub>).</div><div><br></div><img src=""empirical-formula-lab-n.jpg""></div>"
"A compound's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>empirical</b></span> <b>formula </b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;most&#x20;simplified&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;ratio"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> between its elements."	"A compound's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>empirical</b></span> <b>formula </b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>most simplified</i>&nbsp;ratio</span> between its elements.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""Most simplified"" meaning it cannot be simplified any further (like C<sub>3</sub>H<sub>8</sub>).</div><div><br></div><img src=""empirical-formula-lab-n.jpg""></div>"
"A compound's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;molecular&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[molecular/empirical]</span> <b>formula </b>is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">actual&nbsp;<i>number</i></span>&nbsp;of atoms in a compound."	"A compound's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>molecular</b></span> <b>formula </b>is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">actual&nbsp;<i>number</i></span>&nbsp;of atoms in a compound.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""Regular formula""</div><div><br></div><img src=""empirical-formula-lab-n.jpg""></div>"
"A compound's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>molecular</b></span> <b>formula </b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""actual&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;number&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;of atoms in a compound."	"A compound's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>molecular</b></span> <b>formula </b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">actual&nbsp;<i>number</i></span>&nbsp;of atoms in a compound.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>""Regular formula""</div><div><br></div><img src=""empirical-formula-lab-n.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation to find the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;empirical&#x20;formula&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> when given <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;percent&#x20;composition&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of each element:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""8ff9a1b58ba40cc6376c3e84fac8f054.png""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation to find the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>empirical formula</b></span> when given <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>percent composition</u></span> of each element:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""8ff9a1b58ba40cc6376c3e84fac8f054.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- These <u>aren't</u> being multiplied. This only shows how dividing <b>(%)</b> / <b>(molar mass)</b> gives its subscript for the empirical formula.</div><div><br></div><img src=""empirical-formula-lab-n.jpg""></div>"
"<div>To find the <b>empirical formula</b> ratios of elements, divide the element's<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;given&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;percent&#x20;composition"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> by the element's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""molar&#x20;mass"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>To find the <b>empirical formula</b> ratios of elements, divide the element's<i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>given&nbsp;</i>percent composition</span> by the element's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">molar mass</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- These <u>aren't</u> being multiplied. This only shows how dividing <b>(%)</b> / <b>(molar mass)</b> gives its subscript for the empirical formula.</div><div>- Seen below.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ff9a1b58ba40cc6376c3e84fac8f054.png"">&nbsp;<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""empirical-formula-lab-n.jpg""></div>"
"Written next to the element's symbol,&nbsp;the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""mass&#x20;number&#x20;&#x28;A&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is written on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>top</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>&amp; the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""atomic&#x20;number&#x20;&#x28;Z&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is written on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>bottom</i></span> in the&nbsp;<b>standard atomic notation</b>."	"Written next to the element's symbol,&nbsp;the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">mass number (A)</span> is written on <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>top</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>&amp; the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">atomic number (Z)</span> is written on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>bottom</i></span> in the&nbsp;<b>standard atomic notation</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""aee4hsXGId0u73ln8Z7Ad6X482frZ1MTuMpdPxYJTZpEkP-7gUJHriNUzWhf5Pzb9Vqhmi-12ATZlrCNVcbNKU0JZOwrZNMqthlWqWJWuXNCEbMzbr5FBv""></div>"
"Written next to the element's symbol,&nbsp;the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">mass number (A)</span> is written on <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;top&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[top/bottom]</span><i>&nbsp;</i>&amp; the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">atomic number (Z)</span> is written on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;bottom&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[top/bottom]</span> in the&nbsp;<b>standard atomic notation</b>."	"Written next to the element's symbol,&nbsp;the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">mass number (A)</span> is written on <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>top</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i>&amp; the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">atomic number (Z)</span> is written on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>bottom</i></span> in the&nbsp;<b>standard atomic notation</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""aee4hsXGId0u73ln8Z7Ad6X482frZ1MTuMpdPxYJTZpEkP-7gUJHriNUzWhf5Pzb9Vqhmi-12ATZlrCNVcbNKU0JZOwrZNMqthlWqWJWuXNCEbMzbr5FBv""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Non-metals</span> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-/0]</span> <b>electron affinities</b>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Non-metals</span> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positive</i></span> <b>electron affinities</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Metals</b>: negative electron affinity (want to lose electrons)</div><div><b>Non-metals</b>: positive electron affinity (want to gain electrons)</div><div><b>Halogens</b>: zero electron affinity</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""electron_affinity_city_w.png""></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Non&#x2D;metals"" data-ordinal=""2"">[metals/non-metals]</span> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positive</i></span> <b>electron affinities</b>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Non-metals</span> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>positive</i></span> <b>electron affinities</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Metals</b>: negative electron affinity (want to lose electrons)</div><div><b>Non-metals</b>: positive electron affinity (want to gain electrons)</div><div><b>Halogens</b>: zero electron affinity</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""electron_affinity_city_w.png""></div></div>"
"Atoms with the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lowest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[highest/lowest]</span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ionization energies</u></span> react the <i>strongest</i>&nbsp;with <b>water</b>, since they readily donate electrons."	"Atoms with the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lowest</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ionization energies</u></span> react the <i>strongest</i>&nbsp;with <b>water</b>, since they readily donate electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Lower ionization energy means it gives off electrons the easiest... higher reactivity.</div><div>- Ex: <b>Cs </b>is <u>highly</u> reactive with water since it has the lowest ionization energy (bottom left of the periodic table).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div>"
"Atoms with the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lowest</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;ionization&#x20;energies&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> react the <i>strongest</i>&nbsp;with <b>water</b>, since they readily donate electrons."	"Atoms with the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lowest</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>ionization energies</u></span> react the <i>strongest</i>&nbsp;with <b>water</b>, since they readily donate electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Lower ionization energy means it gives off electrons the easiest... higher reactivity.</div><div>- Ex: <b>Cs </b>is <u>highly</u> reactive with water since it has the lowest ionization energy (bottom left of the periodic table).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f38de24231072df639df5147db58f59a1f4dff1.jpg""><br></div>"
"When writing the <b>electron configuration</b> of a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;cation&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cation/anion]</span>, <i>remove </i>the electrons from the orbital with the&nbsp;<i>highest </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">principle quantum numbers (n)</span>."	"When writing the <b>electron configuration</b> of a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cation</u></span>, <i>remove </i>the electrons from the orbital with the&nbsp;<i>highest </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">principle quantum numbers (n)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- If you have a +2 cation, remove (erase) the subshell with the greatest principle quantum number (highest coefficient) to account for the lost electrons.</div>-&nbsp;<a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9uYftghZLEo"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9uYftghZLEo</a><div><br></div><div><img src=""3ebf6667bc1f00d326454b3d2fd26047.png""><br></div></div>"
"When writing the <b>electron configuration</b> of a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cation</u></span>, <i>remove </i>the electrons from the orbital with the&nbsp;<i>highest </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""principle&#x20;quantum&#x20;numbers&#x20;&#x28;n&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"When writing the <b>electron configuration</b> of a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cation</u></span>, <i>remove </i>the electrons from the orbital with the&nbsp;<i>highest </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">principle quantum numbers (n)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- If you have a +2 cation, remove (erase) the subshell with the greatest principle quantum number (highest coefficient) to account for the lost electrons.</div>-&nbsp;<a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9uYftghZLEo"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9uYftghZLEo</a><div><br></div><div><img src=""3ebf6667bc1f00d326454b3d2fd26047.png""><br></div></div>"
"When writing the <b>electron configuration</b> of a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;anion&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[cation/anion]</span>, <i>add </i>electrons to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">shell with the unfilled orbital (usually on the end)</span>."	"When writing the <b>electron configuration</b> of a(n) <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>anion</u></span>, <i>add </i>electrons to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">shell with the unfilled orbital (usually on the end)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- An exception would be if it was more stable half-filling a previous subshell.</div><div>- If you have a -1 anion, add the electron to the subshell with the unfilled orbital (usually the last one).</div>-&nbsp;<a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9uYftghZLEo"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9uYftghZLEo</a><div><br></div><div><img src=""692aa4520bc24af3aa5a1eeeb0c1116c.png""><br></div></div>"
"The electron's <u>angular momentum</u>&nbsp;is referring to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;azimuthal&#x20;&#x28;ℓ&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;quantum number."	"The electron's <u>angular momentum</u>&nbsp;is referring to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>azimuthal (ℓ)</b></span>&nbsp;quantum number.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Azimuthal quantum number (ℓ) <u>also</u> specifies the subshell's <u><b>shape</b></u>... just happens to be referenced by ""angular momentum"" as well.</div><div><br></div><img src=""dcc2ad0f546482fc6a13e61745bfcfa8.png""></div>"
"To calculate the <b>effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b> of an electron in a given shell...<div><br></div><div><b>Z<sub>eff</sub></b> = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;&#x23;protons&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;&#x23;shielding&#x20;electrons&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div>"	"To calculate the <b>effective nuclear charge (Z<sub>eff</sub>)</b> of an electron in a given shell...<div><br></div><div><b>Z<sub>eff</sub></b> = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(#protons)</span> - <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(#shielding electrons)</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Number of <b>valence </b>electrons in <u>irrelevant</u> here.</div><div>- Draw the atomic diagram if it helps. Refer to the table below for the number of electrons in each shell (add up all of the ""shielding"" shells to find the number of shielding electrons).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""effective-nuclear.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ed09d4f78ba86c08974af47718619d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"In an atom, the n=<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1</span> <b>energy shell</b> can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> electrons."	"In an atom, the n=<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1</span> <b>energy shell</b> can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span> electrons.

"In an atom, the n=<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">6</span> <b>energy shell</b> can hold a <i>maximum </i>of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">72</span> electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>2n<sup>2</sup></b>&nbsp;electrons per shell (where n is the principle quantum number)<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""effective-nuclear.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ed09d4f78ba86c08974af47718619d4.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Equivalent&#x20;weight&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;is the <u><i>mass </i>of a compound</u> needed for it to <i>produce </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1 mole of an&nbsp;<u>equivalent</u></span> of some particle of interest (H<sup>+</sup>, OH<sup>-</sup>, etc.)."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Equivalent weight</b></span>&nbsp;is the <u><i>mass </i>of a compound</u> needed for it to <i>produce </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1 mole of an&nbsp;<u>equivalent</u></span> of some particle of interest (H<sup>+</sup>, OH<sup>-</sup>, etc.).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>n</b>&nbsp;= <u>number of equivalents</u> of the particle of interest the main compound has.<br></div><div>- Equivalent is just another way to say the ""number"" of some particle. H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub> has 2 H<sup>+</sup> equivalents, H<sub>3</sub>PO<sub>4</sub> has 3 H<sup>+</sup> equivalents, etc.)</div><div>- Titration shows 2 OH- equivalents are needed to fully neutralize H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>. Gram equivalent weight would be the mass of the OH<sup>-</sup> source needed to give off 2 OH<sup>-</sup> equivalents.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ed17d84c97e9c17ac2ee8b3fe965768f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0687d4a09c4d7dae4871769de23ca84b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Equivalent weight</b></span>&nbsp;is the <u><i>mass </i>of a compound</u> needed for it to <i>produce </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x20;mole&#x20;of&#x20;an&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;equivalent&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of some particle of interest (H<sup>+</sup>, OH<sup>-</sup>, etc.)."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Equivalent weight</b></span>&nbsp;is the <u><i>mass </i>of a compound</u> needed for it to <i>produce </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">1 mole of an&nbsp;<u>equivalent</u></span> of some particle of interest (H<sup>+</sup>, OH<sup>-</sup>, etc.).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>n</b>&nbsp;= <u>number of equivalents</u> of the particle of interest the main compound has.<br></div><div>- Equivalent is just another way to say the ""number"" of some particle. H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub> has 2 H<sup>+</sup> equivalents, H<sub>3</sub>PO<sub>4</sub> has 3 H<sup>+</sup> equivalents, etc.)</div><div>- Titration shows 2 OH- equivalents are needed to fully neutralize H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>. Gram equivalent weight would be the mass of the OH<sup>-</sup> source needed to give off 2 OH<sup>-</sup> equivalents.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ed17d84c97e9c17ac2ee8b3fe965768f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0687d4a09c4d7dae4871769de23ca84b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <i>mass</i>&nbsp;of a compound needed to produce <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x20;mole"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of some <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""equivalent"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""ed17d84c97e9c17ac2ee8b3fe965768f.png""></span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <i>mass</i>&nbsp;of a compound needed to produce <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 mole</span> of some <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">equivalent</span>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""ed17d84c97e9c17ac2ee8b3fe965768f.png""></span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Molar mass</b>: molar mass of the entire compound (H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>).</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>n</b>&nbsp;= <u>number of equivalents</u> of the particle of interest the main compound has.<br></div><div>- Equivalent is just another way to say the ""number"" of some particle. H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub> has 2 H<sup>+</sup> equivalents, H<sub>3</sub>PO<sub>4</sub> has 3 H<sup>+</sup> equivalents, etc.)</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""ed17d84c97e9c17ac2ee8b3fe965768f.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0687d4a09c4d7dae4871769de23ca84b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <i>mass</i>&nbsp;of a compound needed to produce <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 mole</span> of some <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">equivalent</span>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;ed17d84c97e9c17ac2ee8b3fe965768f&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <i>mass</i>&nbsp;of a compound needed to produce <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 mole</span> of some <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">equivalent</span>:</div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""ed17d84c97e9c17ac2ee8b3fe965768f.png""></span><br></div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>n</b> is the <u>number</u> of equivalences (H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub> has 2 H<sup>+</sup> equivalences).&nbsp;</div><div>- Even if you're working with something like a weak base, assume that the reaction goes fully to completion (i.e. H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub> dissociates 100%)</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0687d4a09c4d7dae4871769de23ca84b.png""><br></div></div>"

"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Combination</b></span> <b>reactions </b>are when <i>2</i> reactants join together to form <i>1</i> product (A + B → C).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""e86736a16c84e1d19d9708c21344034f.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Decomposition&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>reactions </b>are when <i>1</i> reactant breaks into <i>2</i> products (A → B + C)."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Decomposition</b></span> <b>reactions </b>are when <i>1</i> reactant breaks into <i>2</i> products (A → B + C).

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""1e4660e4e5c01debe052d9f1c522088d.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Combustion&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>reactions </b>use a <u>hydrocarbon</u> &amp; an <u>oxidant</u> as reactants and <i>always</i> form <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">CO<sub>2</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O</span> as products."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Combustion</b></span> <b>reactions </b>use a <u>hydrocarbon</u> &amp; an <u>oxidant</u> as reactants and <i>always</i> form <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">CO<sub>2</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O</span> as products.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Combustion</b></span> <b>reactions </b>use a <u>hydrocarbon</u> &amp; an <u>oxidant</u> as reactants and <i>always</i> form <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">CO<sub>2</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O</span> as products.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Other elements/compounds (sugars or sulfur) can be used in place of the hydrocarbons as reactants though.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""0ed60b1536f5579a598c87f8939f6267.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Single&#x2D;displacement&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>reactions </b>are where&nbsp;<i>1</i> atom replaces <i>another </i>atom in a compound (AB + C → A + BC)."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Single-displacement</b></span> <b>reactions </b>are where&nbsp;<i>1</i> atom replaces <i>another </i>atom in a compound (AB + C → A + BC).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>This displacement causes one atom to get oxidized, while causing the other to be reduced.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""18c8e75b00f2f1f252ede9f636ca35ed.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Double&#x2D;displacement&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>reactions </b>are where&nbsp;<i>2</i> atoms replace <i>each other&nbsp;</i>in a compound (AB + CD → AC + BD)."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Double-displacement</b></span> <b>reactions </b>are where&nbsp;<i>2</i> atoms replace <i>each other&nbsp;</i>in a compound (AB + CD → AC + BD).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Weak electrolyte</b> since it's weakly ionic and doesn't 100% dissolve in solution.</div><div><br></div><img src=""657ca04e7ace186747ee8e3ba98e832f.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Double</b></span>-<b>displacement reactions </b>result in the <i>formation </i>of a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;weak&#x20;electrolyte&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> &amp; a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;precipitate&#x2F;gas&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Double</b></span>-<b>displacement reactions </b>result in the <i>formation </i>of a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>weak electrolyte</u></span> &amp; a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>precipitate/gas</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Weak electrolyte</b> since it's weakly ionic and doesn't 100% dissolve in solution.</div><div><br></div><img src=""657ca04e7ace186747ee8e3ba98e832f.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Neutralization&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>reactions </b>are types of double-displacement reactions where an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acid</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>base</u></span> react to form a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>salt</i></span> (and usually water)."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Neutralization</b></span> <b>reactions </b>are types of double-displacement reactions where an <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>acid</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>base</u></span> react to form a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>salt</i></span> (and usually water).

<div><img src=""d37a41a0a74919ea02b38958a4035fa5.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""267c0ec1563bca10d67a36ea78fc8429.png""></div>"
"The <i>coefficients &amp; subscripts&nbsp;</i>in balanced chemical equations represent <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""moles"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <i>coefficients &amp; subscripts&nbsp;</i>in balanced chemical equations represent <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>If it says you're given grams for the compounds, use that to find the exact moles of each. Otherwise, look at the coefficients</div><div><br></div><img src=""787844b24142b40cb564a39f4b93ba6b.png""></div>"
"General steps for <b>stoichiometry </b>problems:<div><br></div><div>1. Convert from the <i>given </i>units → <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""moles"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>2. Use the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""mole&#x20;ratio&#x20;&#x28;ratio&#x20;of&#x20;moles&#x20;between&#x20;compounds&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span></div><div>3. Convert from <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""moles"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> → <i>desired </i>units</div>"	"General steps for <b>stoichiometry </b>problems:<div><br></div><div>1. Convert from the <i>given </i>units → <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles</span></div><div>2. Use the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">mole ratio (ratio of moles between compounds)</span></div><div>3. Convert from <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles</span> → <i>desired </i>units</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- That's how you set up a conversion table (like the one below).</div><div>- This goes for any units that can be converted into moles...not just grams.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""197598d50f617062c0287282a8b156ea.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""0f00cb71f2473e44fd887e218c0966ea.png""></div>"
"When determining the <b>limiting reagent</b>, you <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;cannot&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> determine it from only looking at <u>grams</u>."	"When determining the <b>limiting reagent</b>, you <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> determine it from only looking at <u>grams</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Grams <u>do not</u> indicate the limiting reagent... find the moles of each instead (using given grams).</div><div><br></div><img src=""fd582664a5bb62ee4dba3b76e96d0c0d.png""></div>"
"<div>The <b>limiting reagent</b> in a reaction is the reagent that yields the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lowest&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;moles&#x20;of&#x20;product"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>The <b>limiting reagent</b> in a reaction is the reagent that yields the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lowest&nbsp;</i>moles of product</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Use the grams given to find the exact number of moles for each compound.</div><div>Grams <u>do not</u> indicate the limiting reagent... find the moles of each instead (using the given grams).</div><div><br></div><img src=""fd582664a5bb62ee4dba3b76e96d0c0d.png""></div>"
"To find the <u>moles</u> of the <i>remaining </i><b>excess reagent</b>, <i>subtract </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;starting&#x20;moles&#x20;of&#x20;E&#x2E;R&#x2E;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;moles&#x20;of&#x20;L&#x2E;R&#x2E;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"To find the <u>moles</u> of the <i>remaining </i><b>excess reagent</b>, <i>subtract </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(starting moles of E.R.)</span> - <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(moles of L.R.)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Subtract by the moles of L.R., since we're assuming 100% of the L.R. reacts with some moles of the E.R., leaving a little ""<u>excess</u>"" over.</div><div>- Grams <u>do not</u> indicate the limiting reagent... find the moles of each instead.</div><div><br></div><img src=""fd582664a5bb62ee4dba3b76e96d0c0d.png""></div>"
"<b>Oxidation </b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;loss&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[gain/loss]</span> of electrons."	"<b>Oxidation </b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>loss</i></span> of electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>OIL RIG</b></div>"
"<b>Reduction&nbsp;</b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;gain&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[gain/loss]</span> of electrons."	"<b>Reduction&nbsp;</b>is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>gain</i></span> of electrons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>OIL RIG</b></div>"
"The <b>oxidation numbers</b> of <i>free </i>elements (N<sub>2</sub>, He, P<sub>4</sub>, O<sub>2</sub>) are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""zero"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>oxidation numbers</b> of <i>free </i>elements (N<sub>2</sub>, He, P<sub>4</sub>, O<sub>2</sub>) are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">zero</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Peroxides are specifically&nbsp;<u>anionic</u>&nbsp;O<sub>2</sub>'s. Most O<sub>2</sub>'s (like SO<sub>2</sub>) will have oxygen atoms&nbsp;<u>each</u>&nbsp;as -2.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-611670e5fdce0831277fddeb7c29936e5e73f4cd.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <b>oxidation numbers</b>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<i>monoatomic </i>ions (Na<sup>+</sup>, Ca<sup>2+</sup>) are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""the&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;same&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;as&#x20;their&#x20;charges"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>oxidation numbers</b>&nbsp;of&nbsp;<i>monoatomic </i>ions (Na<sup>+</sup>, Ca<sup>2+</sup>) are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">the&nbsp;<i>same </i>as their charges</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Peroxides are specifically&nbsp;<u>anionic</u>&nbsp;O<sub>2</sub>'s. Most O<sub>2</sub>'s (like SO<sub>2</sub>) will have oxygen atoms&nbsp;<u>each</u>&nbsp;as -2.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-611670e5fdce0831277fddeb7c29936e5e73f4cd.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <b>oxidation numbers</b>&nbsp;of <u>group 1</u> elements&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2B;1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <b>oxidation numbers</b>&nbsp;of <u>group 1</u> elements&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">+1</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-611670e5fdce0831277fddeb7c29936e5e73f4cd.jpg""></div>"
"A compound's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;empirical&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>formula </b>can be calculated by comparing the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">mole ratios</span> of <i>each </i>element when you're <i>not </i><u>given percentages</u>."	"A compound's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>empirical</b></span> <b>formula </b>can be calculated by comparing the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">mole ratios</span> of <i>each </i>element when you're <i>not </i><u>given percentages</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Using the given grams of each, you can find the moles of each atom. Look at the ratio to then determine the empirical formula.</div>"
"A compound's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>empirical</b></span> <b>formula </b>can be calculated by comparing the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""mole&#x20;ratios"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of <i>each </i>element when you're <i>not </i><u>given percentages</u>."	"A compound's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>empirical</b></span> <b>formula </b>can be calculated by comparing the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">mole ratios</span> of <i>each </i>element when you're <i>not </i><u>given percentages</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Using the given grams of each, you can find the moles of each atom. Look at the ratio to then determine the empirical formula.</div>"
"To find the <u>mass</u> of the <i>remaining </i><b>excess reagent</b>, subtract <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;starting&#x20;grams&#x20;of&#x20;E&#x2E;R&#x2E;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;grams&#x20;of&#x20;E&#x2E;R&#x2E;&#x20;consumed&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"To find the <u>mass</u> of the <i>remaining </i><b>excess reagent</b>, subtract <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(starting grams of E.R.)</span> - <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(grams of E.R. consumed)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Find grams consumed by plugging in its number of moles consumed into MW=g/mol.</div><div>- Grams&nbsp;<u>do not</u>&nbsp;indicate the limiting reagent... find the moles of each instead to determine which is the L.R. or E.R. in the first place.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""fd582664a5bb62ee4dba3b76e96d0c0d.png""></div>"
"<div>How could you calculate <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>theoretical yield</b></span>?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;Substitute&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;moles&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;limiting&#x20;reagent&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;in&#x20;for&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;u&gt;moles&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;product&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x2E;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"<div>How could you calculate <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>theoretical yield</b></span>?<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>Substitute</i> the <u>moles of the limiting reagent</u> <i>in for</i> the <u>moles of the product</u>.</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Use the moles in the product's MW = g/mol expression and solve for grams.</div><div><br></div><img src=""601a4138a32a8efde1b9440f821a2ddc.png""></div>"
"The formula for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">hydrochloric acid</span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""HCl"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The formula for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">hydrochloric acid</span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">HCl</span>.

"<div>Equation for <b>molality</b>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;ba4f76265878c171b6eb38caf4940a3f&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for <b>molality</b>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""ba4f76265878c171b6eb38caf4940a3f.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Instead of molarity (moles/L), <u>molality</u> is <b>moles/kg</b>.</div>"
"In <b>electrochemical cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;anode&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span> is where <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>oxidation</i></span> occurs."	"In <b>electrochemical cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>anode</u></span> is where <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>oxidation</i></span> occurs.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- This is true for galvanic, electrolytic, and concentration cells.<div>- Oxidation is the <b>loss</b> of electrons. Remember that electrons travel from anode → cathode (loses electrons).</div></div>"
"In <b>electrochemical cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>anode</u></span> is where <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;oxidation&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[oxi./red.]</span> occurs."	"In <b>electrochemical cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>anode</u></span> is where <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>oxidation</i></span> occurs.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- This is true for galvanic, electrolytic, and concentration cells.<div>- Oxidation is the <b>loss</b> of electrons. Remember that electrons travel from anode → cathode (loses electrons).</div></div>"
"In <b>electrochemical cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;cathode&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span> is where <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>reduction</i></span> occurs."	"In <b>electrochemical cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cathode</u></span> is where <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>reduction</i></span> occurs.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- This is true for galvanic, electrolytic, and concentration cells.<div>- Reduction is the&nbsp;<b>gain</b>&nbsp;of electrons. Remember that electrons travel from anode → cathode (receives electrons).</div></div>"
"In <b>electrochemical cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cathode</u></span> is where <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;reduction&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[oxi./red.]</span> occurs."	"In <b>electrochemical cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cathode</u></span> is where <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>reduction</i></span> occurs.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- This is true for galvanic, electrolytic, and concentration cells.<div>- Reduction is the&nbsp;<b>gain</b>&nbsp;of electrons. Remember that electrons travel from anode → cathode (receives electrons).</div></div>"
"In electrolysis, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;galvanic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>cells </b>utilize <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spontaneous</i></span> redox reactions - producing a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">positive</span> emf."	"In electrolysis, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>galvanic</b></span> <b>cells </b>utilize <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spontaneous</i></span> redox reactions - producing a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">positive</span> emf.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>+ε; -ΔG&nbsp;</div><div>- These are the electrolytic cells used as batteries (since they must be spontaneous in order to generate the power supply for the emf).</div><div>- The <b>electromotive force (ε)</b> &amp; <b>ΔG</b> always have <u>opposite</u> signs.</div><div><br></div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div>"
"In electrolysis, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>galvanic</b></span> <b>cells </b>utilize <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;spontaneous&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[non-spont./spont.]</span> redox reactions - producing a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">positive</span> emf."	"In electrolysis, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>galvanic</b></span> <b>cells </b>utilize <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spontaneous</i></span> redox reactions - producing a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">positive</span> emf.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>+ε; -ΔG&nbsp;</div><div>- These are the electrolytic cells used as batteries (since they must be spontaneous in order to generate the power supply for the emf).</div><div>- The <b>electromotive force (ε)</b> &amp; <b>ΔG</b> always have <u>opposite</u> signs.</div><div><br></div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div>"
"In electrolysis, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>galvanic</b></span> <b>cells </b>utilize <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spontaneous</i></span> redox reactions - producing a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""positive"" data-ordinal=""3"">[+/-]</span> emf."	"In electrolysis, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>galvanic</b></span> <b>cells </b>utilize <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>spontaneous</i></span> redox reactions - producing a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">positive</span> emf.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>+ε; -ΔG&nbsp;</div><div>- These are the electrolytic cells used as batteries (since they must be spontaneous in order to generate the power supply for the emf).</div><div>- The <b>electromotive force (ε)</b> &amp; <b>ΔG</b> always have <u>opposite</u> signs.</div><div><br></div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div>"
"In <i>all </i>electrochemical cells, the movement of <u>electrons</u>&nbsp;go from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""anode"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cathode"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"In <i>all </i>electrochemical cells, the movement of <u>electrons</u>&nbsp;go from the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">anode</span> → <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cathode</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The current points in the direction of the cathode → anode though (current represents the theoretical direction positive charges would go)</div><div>- Below shows an example of a <b>galvanic cell</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In <b>galvanic cells</b>, the <u>anode</u> uses a Zinc (Zn) bar with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ZnSO&lt;sub&gt;4&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> ions in solution, which get <i>oxidized</i> into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Zn&lt;sup&gt;2&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> while their electrons flow towards the cathode.</div>"	"<div>In <b>galvanic cells</b>, the <u>anode</u> uses a Zinc (Zn) bar with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ZnSO<sub>4</sub></span> ions in solution, which get <i>oxidized</i> into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Zn<sup>2+</sup></span> while their electrons flow towards the cathode.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Electrons travel through the <u>copper wire</u> to reach the cathode (Cu (s))</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""098cf7ba35a1957a943cf3c8c111ff96.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ebfa5a776631ba0a62e93d2dcfcd66a.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In&nbsp;<b>galvanic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cathode</u></span>&nbsp;uses a Copper (Cu) bar with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""CuSO&lt;sub&gt;4&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> ions in solution, which get&nbsp;<i>reduced </i>into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Cu&#x20;&#x28;s&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> after&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>receiving</i>&nbsp;the anode's electrons</span>.</div>"	"<div>In&nbsp;<b>galvanic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cathode</u></span>&nbsp;uses a Copper (Cu) bar with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">CuSO<sub>4</sub></span> ions in solution, which get&nbsp;<i>reduced </i>into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Cu (s)</span> after&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>receiving</i>&nbsp;the anode's electrons</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Electrons travel through the <u>copper wire</u> to reach the cathode (Cu (s)) from the anode (Zn (s)).</div><div>- Reduction from Cu<sup>2+</sup> → Cu (s) causes copper to solidify on the cathode itself.</div><div><br></div><div><div><img src=""098cf7ba35a1957a943cf3c8c111ff96.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ebfa5a776631ba0a62e93d2dcfcd66a.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div></div></div>"
"<div>For <b>redox reactions</b> in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;acidic&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[acidic/basic]</span> solutions, add <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sup>+</sup></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O</span> to balance out O and H atoms.</div>"	"<div>For <b>redox reactions</b> in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>acidic</i></u></span> solutions, add <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sup>+</sup></span> and <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O</span> to balance out O and H atoms.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>For all redox problems, make sure the electrons on both half reaction has the same coefficient.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""slide_19.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>For <b>redox reactions</b> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>acidic</i></u></span> solutions, add <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""H&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> and <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""H&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;O"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> to balance out O and H atoms.</div>"	"<div>For <b>redox reactions</b> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><u><i>acidic</i></u></span> solutions, add <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sup>+</sup></span> and <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O</span> to balance out O and H atoms.</div>
