<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;solubility&#x20;product&#x20;constant&#x20;&#x28;K&lt;sub&gt;sp&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""d08e3ab7711456af09762ff2d087c7ea.png""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>solubility product constant (K<sub>sp</sub>)</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""d08e3ab7711456af09762ff2d087c7ea.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""solubility&#x20;product&#x20;constant&#x20;&#x28;K&lt;sub&gt;sp&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> measures the <i>solubility </i>of a compound in solution.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">solubility product constant (K<sub>sp</sub>)</span> measures the <i>solubility </i>of a compound in solution.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Only include the ions in the constant - <u>not</u> the solids.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>K</b><sub style=""font-weight: bold;"">sp </sub><b>(solubility constant)</b></span>&nbsp;<i>increases </i>for <u>non-gases</u> when temperature is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increased&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increased/decreased]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>K</b><sub style=""font-weight: bold;"">sp </sub><b>(solubility constant)</b></span>&nbsp;<i>increases </i>for <u>non-gases</u> when temperature is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increased</i></span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Since most <u>non-gas</u> substances are <b>endothermic</b> when they dissolve.</div><div>- Even though Ksp is a constant, its magnitude can change from changes in temperature.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>K</b><sub style=""font-weight: bold;"">sp </sub><b>(solubility constant)</b></span>&nbsp;<i>increases </i>for <u>gases</u> when temperature is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreased&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increased/decreased]</span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>K</b><sub style=""font-weight: bold;"">sp </sub><b>(solubility constant)</b></span>&nbsp;<i>increases </i>for <u>gases</u> when temperature is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreased</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...since <u>gas</u> substances are <b>exothermic</b> when they dissolve (exothermic is favored at low temperatures).</div><div><br></div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> the <b>K<sub>sp</sub></b> of a substance in solution, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> its <u>solubility</u>.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> the <b>K<sub>sp</sub></b> of a substance in solution, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> its <u>solubility</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> the <b>K<sub>sp</sub></b> of a substance in solution, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[higher/lower]</span> its <u>solubility</u>.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> the <b>K<sub>sp</sub></b> of a substance in solution, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> its <u>solubility</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> the <b>K<sub>sp</sub></b> of a substance in solution, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> its <u>solubility</u>.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span> the <b>K<sub>sp</sub></b> of a substance in solution, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> its <u>solubility</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span> the <b>K<sub>sp</sub></b> of a substance in solution, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[higher/lower]</span> its <u>solubility</u>.<br></div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span> the <b>K<sub>sp</sub></b> of a substance in solution, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>lower</i></span> its <u>solubility</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>For <u>gases</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[higher/lower]</span> <u>pressures</u> result in <i>higher </i><b>K<sub>sp</sub>'s </b>(solubility constant).</div>"	"<div>For <u>gases</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> <u>pressures</u> result in <i>higher </i><b>K<sub>sp</sub>'s </b>(solubility constant).</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>According to <b>Henry's law</b>, increased pressure results in increased gas soubility (i.e. concentration of gas particles in solution).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ion&#x20;product&#x20;&#x28;IP&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in <u>solubility equilibrium</u> problems is <i>analogous </i>to the reaction quotient (Q)."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ion product (IP)</b></span> in <u>solubility equilibrium</u> problems is <i>analogous </i>to the reaction quotient (Q).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Describes where the solubility is currently at relative to the final solubility equilibrium (K<sub>sp</sub>).</div><div><br></div><img src=""2c7d40a6e50860616ae77cf5f33e6672.png""></div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;ion&#x20;product&#x20;&#x28;IP&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> describes the <i>current </i>amount of ions&nbsp;<u>dissolved in solution</u> <i>before </i>they reach the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">solubility equilibrium (K<sub>sp</sub>)</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>ion product (IP)</b></span> describes the <i>current </i>amount of ions&nbsp;<u>dissolved in solution</u> <i>before </i>they reach the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">solubility equilibrium (K<sub>sp</sub>)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>Same exact equation as K<sub style="""">sp</sub></b> (multiplying the concentrations of the separated ionic species), except it only describes the solubility at that point in time relative to the equilibrium.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2c7d40a6e50860616ae77cf5f33e6672.png""></div>"
"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;smaller&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[larger/smaller]</span> than K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction will proceed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>forward</u></span> &amp; lead to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>dissolution</u></span>.</div>"	"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i></span> than K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction will proceed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>forward</u></span> &amp; lead to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>dissolution</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Continues to dissolve to reach its equilibrium point.</div><div>- <b>Same exact equation as K<sub style="""">sp</sub></b> (multiplying the concentrations of the separated ionic species), except it only describes the solubility at that point in time relative to the equilibrium.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d32c17abb522357b4ae4b34db89d06d0152686b1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i></span> than K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction will proceed <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;forward&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[forward/backward]</span> &amp; lead to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;dissolution&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[diss./precip.]</span>.</div>"	"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>smaller</i></span> than K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction will proceed <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>forward</u></span> &amp; lead to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>dissolution</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Continues to dissolve to reach its equilibrium point.</div><div>- <b>Same exact equation as K<sub style="""">sp</sub></b> (multiplying the concentrations of the separated ionic species), except it only describes the solubility at that point in time relative to the equilibrium.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d32c17abb522357b4ae4b34db89d06d0152686b1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;larger&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[larger/smaller]</span> than K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction will proceed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>backward</u></span> &amp; lead to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>precipitation</u></span>.</div>"	"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> than K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction will proceed <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>backward</u></span> &amp; lead to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>precipitation</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Precipitates to reach its equilibrium point.</div><div>- <b>Same exact equation as K<sub style="""">sp</sub></b> (multiplying the concentrations of the separated ionic species), except it only describes the solubility at that point in time relative to the equilibrium.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d32c17abb522357b4ae4b34db89d06d0152686b1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> than K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction will proceed <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;backward&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[forward/backward]</span> &amp; lead to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;precipitation&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[diss./precip.]</span>.</div>"	"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> than K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction will proceed <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>backward</u></span> &amp; lead to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>precipitation</u></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Precipitates to reach its equilibrium point.</div><div>- <b>Same exact equation as K<sub style="""">sp</sub></b> (multiplying the concentrations of the separated ionic species), except it only describes the solubility at that point in time relative to the equilibrium.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d32c17abb522357b4ae4b34db89d06d0152686b1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;equal&#x20;to&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction is <i>saturated</i> at its <u>equilibrium</u> position.</div>"	"<div>When the <b>ion product (IP)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>equal to</i></span> K<sub>sp</sub>, the reaction is <i>saturated</i> at its <u>equilibrium</u> position.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Same exact equation as K<sub>sp</sub></b> (multiplying the concentrations of the separated ionic species), except it only describes the solubility at that point in time relative to the equilibrium.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-d32c17abb522357b4ae4b34db89d06d0152686b1.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Ions are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[more/less]</span> <u>soluble</u> as <b>coordinate complexes</b> in solution than they are on their own due to the high number of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">dipole-dipole interactions</span>."	"Ions are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>more</i></span> <u>soluble</u> as <b>coordinate complexes</b> in solution than they are on their own due to the high number of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">dipole-dipole interactions</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"Ions are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>more</i></span> <u>soluble</u> as <b>coordinate complexes</b> in solution than they are on their own due to the high number of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""dipole&#x2D;dipole&#x20;interactions"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"Ions are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>more</i></span> <u>soluble</u> as <b>coordinate complexes</b> in solution than they are on their own due to the high number of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">dipole-dipole interactions</span>.

"The <b>solubility</b> of a compound is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreased&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increased/decreased]</span> when it's placed in solution with a <u>common ion</u> (i.e. trying to dissolve PbCl<sub>2</sub> in a solution of KCl)."	"The <b>solubility</b> of a compound is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreased</i></span> when it's placed in solution with a <u>common ion</u> (i.e. trying to dissolve PbCl<sub>2</sub> in a solution of KCl).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because LeChatelier's principle says the other side of the reaction will be favored due to the presence of excessl Cl</div>-&nbsp;<a href=""https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pb-wsUpPuI8"">https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pb-wsUpPuI8</a><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-168ec39326798c0f6f0115640d0930f6ed06beb9.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Colligative&#x20;properties&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">physical</span> properties in solution that <i>only </i>rely on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>number&nbsp;</i>of dissolved particles</u></span> but not<i> </i>on the <i>specific</i> identities of those particles."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Colligative properties</b></span>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">physical</span> properties in solution that <i>only </i>rely on the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>number&nbsp;</i>of dissolved particles</u></span> but not<i> </i>on the <i>specific</i> identities of those particles.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Ex: osmotic pressure.<div>- The ability of a substance to boil, freeze, or vaporize has <u>nothing</u> to do with the chemical properties of the substance (like its reactivity).</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Colligative properties</b></span>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">physical</span> properties in solution that <i>only </i>rely on the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;&lt;i&gt;number&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;of&#x20;dissolved&#x20;particles&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> but not<i> </i>on the <i>specific</i> identities of those particles."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Colligative properties</b></span>&nbsp;are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">physical</span> properties in solution that <i>only </i>rely on the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>number&nbsp;</i>of dissolved particles</u></span> but not<i> </i>on the <i>specific</i> identities of those particles.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- Ex: osmotic pressure.<div>- The ability of a substance to boil, freeze, or vaporize has <u>nothing</u> to do with the chemical properties of the substance (like its reactivity).</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Raoult&#x27;s&#x20;law&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[law]</span> says that as you <u>dissolve</u> <i>more </i>solutes in a solvent, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>vapor pressure</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Raoult's law</b></span> says that as you <u>dissolve</u> <i>more </i>solutes in a solvent, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>vapor pressure</u></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...becuase solute molecules <u>block</u> solvent molecules from evaporating into a gas while <u>not</u> blocking the ability of the gas to condense back into the liquid.</div><div>-&nbsp;<u style=""font-weight: bold;"">Vapor pressure depression</u></div><div>- <b>P<sub>A</sub></b> = vapor pressure of solvent; <b>X<sub>A</sub></b> = mole fraction of solvent; <b>P<sub>A</sub>°</b> = vapor pressure in pure state (i.e. what it could have been).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb.png""><br><a href=""https://gyazo.com/05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb"">https://gyazo.com/05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb</a><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Raoult's law</b></span> says that as you <u>dissolve</u> <i>more </i>solutes in a solvent, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;vapor&#x20;pressure&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[colligative property]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decreases&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[increases/decreases]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Raoult's law</b></span> says that as you <u>dissolve</u> <i>more </i>solutes in a solvent, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>vapor pressure</u></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>decreases</i></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...becuase solute molecules <u>block</u> solvent molecules from evaporating into a gas while <u>not</u> blocking the ability of the gas to condense back into the liquid.</div><div>-&nbsp;<u style=""font-weight: bold;"">Vapor pressure depression</u></div><div>- <b>P<sub>A</sub></b> = vapor pressure of solvent; <b>X<sub>A</sub></b> = mole fraction of solvent; <b>P<sub>A</sub>°</b> = vapor pressure in pure state (i.e. what it could have been).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb.png""><br><a href=""https://gyazo.com/05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb"">https://gyazo.com/05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb</a><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>vapor pressure depression</b></span> after adding <i>solutes </i>to a solvent (<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Raoult's law</span>):</div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>vapor pressure depression</b></span> after adding <i>solutes </i>to a solvent (<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Raoult's law</span>):</div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""></span><br></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>P<sub>A</sub></b>&nbsp;= vapor pressure of solvent;&nbsp;<b>X<sub>A</sub></b>&nbsp;= mole fraction of solvent;&nbsp;<b>P<sub>A</sub>°</b>&nbsp;= vapor pressure in pure solution (i.e. what it could have been had it been the only component in solution).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;vapor&#x20;pressure&#x20;depression&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[colligative property]</span> after adding <i>solutes </i>to a solvent (<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Raoult&#x27;s&#x20;law"" data-ordinal=""2"">[law]</span>):</div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""></span><br></div></div>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>vapor pressure depression</b></span> after adding <i>solutes </i>to a solvent (<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">Raoult's law</span>):</div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""></span><br></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>P<sub>A</sub></b>&nbsp;= vapor pressure of solvent;&nbsp;<b>X<sub>A</sub></b>&nbsp;= mole fraction of solvent;&nbsp;<b>P<sub>A</sub>°</b>&nbsp;= vapor pressure in pure solution (i.e. what it could have been had it been the only component in solution).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb.png""><br></div></div>"
"Adding solute to a solvent leads to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;vapor&#x20;pressure&#x20;depression&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> due to solute molecules <i>blocking </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">evaporation</span> but <i>not</i> blocking <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">condensation (back to the liquid)</span>."	"Adding solute to a solvent leads to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>vapor pressure depression</b></span> due to solute molecules <i>blocking </i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">evaporation</span> but <i>not</i> blocking <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">condensation (back to the liquid)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Overall makes it harder for the vapor pressure to build up.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>P<sub>A</sub></b>&nbsp;= vapor pressure of solvent;&nbsp;<b>X<sub>A</sub></b>&nbsp;= mole fraction of solvent;&nbsp;<b>P<sub>A</sub>°</b>&nbsp;= vapor pressure in pure state (i.e. what it could have been).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb.png""><br></div></div>"
"Adding solute to a solvent leads to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>vapor pressure depression</b></span> due to solute molecules <i>blocking </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""evaporation"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> but <i>not</i> blocking <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""condensation&#x20;&#x28;back&#x20;to&#x20;the&#x20;liquid&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"Adding solute to a solvent leads to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>vapor pressure depression</b></span> due to solute molecules <i>blocking </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">evaporation</span> but <i>not</i> blocking <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">condensation (back to the liquid)</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Overall makes it harder for the vapor pressure to build up.</div><div>-&nbsp;<b>P<sub>A</sub></b>&nbsp;= vapor pressure of solvent;&nbsp;<b>X<sub>A</sub></b>&nbsp;= mole fraction of solvent;&nbsp;<b>P<sub>A</sub>°</b>&nbsp;= vapor pressure in pure state (i.e. what it could have been).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""e801c1d58f7c022e2a484076011c7666.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""05370f44800dca1ddf77c6cb9613accb.png""><br></div></div>"
"<u>Vapor pressure depression</u> leads to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;boiling&#x20;point&#x20;elevation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, since <i>diminishing </i>vapor pressure's ability to build up makes it<i> harder</i> for solutions to reach a boil - requiring higher temperatures."	"<u>Vapor pressure depression</u> leads to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>boiling point elevation</b></span>, since <i>diminishing </i>vapor pressure's ability to build up makes it<i> harder</i> for solutions to reach a boil - requiring higher temperatures.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Boiling occurs when vapor pressure = atmospheric pressure.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd.png""></div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>boiling point elevation</b></span> of a solution with <i>dissolved </i>solute in it compared to a pure solution:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>boiling point elevation</b></span> of a solution with <i>dissolved </i>solute in it compared to a pure solution:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>ΔT<sub>b</sub> </b>= <i>increase </i>in boiling point; <b>i</b> = Van't Hoff factor (<u>number</u> of particles the compound dissolves into...i=2 for NaCl); <b>K<sub>b</sub> </b>= constant for a particlar solvent; <b>m</b> = molality (mol/kg).</div>"
"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;boiling&#x20;point&#x20;elevation&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> of a solution with <i>dissolved </i>solute in it compared to a pure solution:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd.png""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>boiling point elevation</b></span> of a solution with <i>dissolved </i>solute in it compared to a pure solution:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd.png""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>ΔT<sub>b</sub> </b>= <i>increase </i>in boiling point; <b>i</b> = Van't Hoff factor (<u>number</u> of particles the compound dissolves into...i=2 for NaCl); <b>K<sub>b</sub> </b>= constant for a particlar solvent; <b>m</b> = molality (mol/kg).</div>"
"<div>In the below equation for <b>boiling point elevation</b> due to <i>dissolved</i> solutes in solution, <b>i</b>&nbsp;is the Van't Hoff factor, which is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;number&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;of&#x20;particles"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> the solute splits into (i=2 for NaCl).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd.png""><br></div>"	"<div>In the below equation for <b>boiling point elevation</b> due to <i>dissolved</i> solutes in solution, <b>i</b>&nbsp;is the Van't Hoff factor, which is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>number</u> of particles</span> the solute splits into (i=2 for NaCl).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd.png""><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>ΔT<sub>b</sub> </b>= <i>increase </i>in boiling point; <b>i</b> = Van't Hoff factor (<u>number</u> of particles the compound dissolves into...i=2 for NaCl); <b>K<sub>b</sub> </b>= constant for a particlar solvent; <b>m</b> = molarity.</div>"
"<div>In the below equation for <b>boiling point elevation</b> due to <i>dissolved</i> solutes in solution, <b>m</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""molality&#x20;&#x28;mol&#x2F;kg&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of the solution.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd.png""><br></div>"	"<div>In the below equation for <b>boiling point elevation</b> due to <i>dissolved</i> solutes in solution, <b>m</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">molality (mol/kg)</span> of the solution.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""1e8c8a256d6c03d8d1e7bf7d8b2122cd.png""><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>ΔT<sub>b</sub> </b>= <i>increase </i>in boiling point; <b>i</b> = Van't Hoff factor (<u>number</u> of particles the compound dissolves into...i=2 for NaCl); <b>K<sub>b</sub> </b>= constant for a particlar solvent; <b>m</b> = molality (mol/kg).</div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Freezing&#x20;point&#x20;depression&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> occurs when solute particles in solution <i>interfere </i>with the ability for compounds to form <u>lattice structures</u>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Freezing point depression</b></span> occurs when solute particles in solution <i>interfere </i>with the ability for compounds to form <u>lattice structures</u>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Even lower temperatures are needed to freeze, in this case.</div><div>- Δ<b>T<sub>f</sub></b> = freezing point depression; <b>i </b>= Van't hoff factor (<u>number</u> of particles the dissolved compound splits into...i=2 for NaCl); <b>K<sub>f</sub></b> = constant (given); <b>m</b> = molarity</div><div><br></div><img src=""396da012cca358efc19f5cea11f91c6e.png""></div>"
"Equation for the <b>freezing point depression</b> that occurs when solute particles <i>interfere </i>with the ability for a compound to form <u>lattice structures</u> in solution:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;396da012cca358efc19f5cea11f91c6e&#x2E;png&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Equation for the <b>freezing point depression</b> that occurs when solute particles <i>interfere </i>with the ability for a compound to form <u>lattice structures</u> in solution:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><img src=""396da012cca358efc19f5cea11f91c6e.png""></span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <u>Same</u> equation as <b>boiling point elevation</b>.</div><div>- Δ<b>T<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= freezing point depression;&nbsp;<b>i&nbsp;</b>= Van't hoff factor (<u>number</u>&nbsp;of particles the dissolved compound splits into...i=2 for NaCl);&nbsp;<b>K<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= constant (given);&nbsp;<b>m</b>&nbsp;= molality (mol/kg).</div></div>"
"<b>Freezing point depression</b> makes it <i>harder </i>for lattice structures to form in solution - forcing liquids to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""draw&#x20;heat&#x20;&lt;i&gt;out&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;from&#x20;the&#x20;solution"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;to <u>crystallize</u>."	"<b>Freezing point depression</b> makes it <i>harder </i>for lattice structures to form in solution - forcing liquids to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">draw heat <i>out </i>from the solution</span>&nbsp;to <u>crystallize</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Freezing point depression ultimately makes the solution colder (since more heat is drawn out from solution to crystallize due to the lower freezing point).</div><div>- Δ<b>T<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= freezing point depression;&nbsp;<b>i&nbsp;</b>= Van't hoff factor (<u>number</u>&nbsp;of particles the dissolved compound splits into...i=2 for NaCl);&nbsp;<b>K<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= constant (given);&nbsp;<b>m</b>&nbsp;= molality (mol/kg).<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""396da012cca358efc19f5cea11f91c6e.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In the equation for <u>osmotic pressure</u>, <b>M</b> is the solution's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""molarity&#x20;&#x28;mol&#x2F;L&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1a3cf71e76086fb3b401ee5a4de9f559.png""><br>"	"<div>In the equation for <u>osmotic pressure</u>, <b>M</b> is the solution's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">molarity (mol/L)</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1a3cf71e76086fb3b401ee5a4de9f559.png""><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Don't confuse this with molality!</div><div>- In freezing point depression &amp; boiling point elevation, 'm' represented molality (mol/kg). For osmotic pressure though, it represents <b><u>molarity (mol/L)</u></b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""b7d8b305faa272df98fccbb5b5e7bdad.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In the equation for <u>osmotic pressure</u>, <b>R</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ideal&#x20;gas&#x20;constant"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1a3cf71e76086fb3b401ee5a4de9f559.png"">"	"<div>In the equation for <u>osmotic pressure</u>, <b>R</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ideal gas constant</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""1a3cf71e76086fb3b401ee5a4de9f559.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""b7d8b305faa272df98fccbb5b5e7bdad.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Arrhenius&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>acids </b>are compounds that dissociate in solution to form an excess of H<sup>+</sup> protons."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Arrhenius</b></span> <b>acids </b>are compounds that dissociate in solution to form an excess of H<sup>+</sup> protons.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is the most restrictive definition of an acid, and it only works in aqueous solutions.</div>-&nbsp;<b>Arrhenius bases</b> in contrast form an excess of <b>OH</b><sup>-</sup> in solution.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Arrhenius&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>bases&nbsp;</b>are compounds that dissociate in solution to form an excess of OH<sup>-</sup> ions."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Arrhenius</b></span> <b>bases&nbsp;</b>are compounds that dissociate in solution to form an excess of OH<sup>-</sup> ions.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is the most restrictive definition of a base, and it only works in aqueous solutions<br></div>-&nbsp;<b>Arrhenius acids</b>&nbsp;in contrast form an excess of <b>H<sup>+</sup></b>&nbsp;in solution.</div>"
"The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Lewis&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> definition for acids &amp; bases is the <i>most </i><u>inclusive</u>."	"The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Lewis</b></span> definition for acids &amp; bases is the <i>most </i><u>inclusive</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"In galvanic cells, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;salt&#x20;bridges&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> allow for the current to <i>continue</i> running by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>neutralizing the charges</u></span> on either side <i>after</i> becoming oxidized/reduced."	"In galvanic cells, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>salt bridges</b></span> allow for the current to <i>continue</i> running by <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>neutralizing the charges</u></span> on either side <i>after</i> becoming oxidized/reduced.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Anode</b> side slowly accumulates a <u>positive</u> charge (since it's losing electrons) - neutralized by the Cl<sup>-</sup> in the KCl ions in the salt bridge.</div><div>- <b>Cathode</b> side slowly accumulates a <u>negative</u> charge (since it's gaining electrons) - neutralized by the K<sup>+</sup> in the KCl ions in the salt bridge.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""098cf7ba35a1957a943cf3c8c111ff96.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ebfa5a776631ba0a62e93d2dcfcd66a.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div></div>"
"In galvanic cells, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>salt bridges</b></span> allow for the current to <i>continue</i> running by <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;neutralizing&#x20;the&#x20;charges&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> on either side <i>after</i> becoming oxidized/reduced."	"In galvanic cells, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>salt bridges</b></span> allow for the current to <i>continue</i> running by <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>neutralizing the charges</u></span> on either side <i>after</i> becoming oxidized/reduced.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Anode</b> side slowly accumulates a <u>positive</u> charge (since it's losing electrons) - neutralized by the Cl<sup>-</sup> in the KCl ions in the salt bridge.</div><div>- <b>Cathode</b> side slowly accumulates a <u>negative</u> charge (since it's gaining electrons) - neutralized by the K<sup>+</sup> in the KCl ions in the salt bridge.<br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""098cf7ba35a1957a943cf3c8c111ff96.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ebfa5a776631ba0a62e93d2dcfcd66a.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div></div>"
"<div>In galvanic cells, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;anode&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span> slowly becomes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>positively</i></span> charged due its oxidation reactions, which the <u>salt bridge</u> <i>neutralizes</i> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Cl<sup>-</sup></span> ions.</div>"	"<div>In galvanic cells, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>anode</b></span> slowly becomes <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>positively</i></span> charged due its oxidation reactions, which the <u>salt bridge</u> <i>neutralizes</i> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">Cl<sup>-</sup></span> ions.</div>

"In galvanic cells, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;plating&#x2F;galvanization&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is when aqueous ions become reduced&nbsp;to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">solids</span> on the cathode&nbsp;- causing them to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>precipitate</u> on the cathode bar</span>."	"In galvanic cells, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>plating/galvanization</b></span> is when aqueous ions become reduced&nbsp;to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">solids</span> on the cathode&nbsp;- causing them to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>precipitate</u> on the cathode bar</span>.

<div>Example is taken from this galvanic cell.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""098cf7ba35a1957a943cf3c8c111ff96.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ebfa5a776631ba0a62e93d2dcfcd66a.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Electrolytic&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[galvanic/elect.]</span>&nbsp;<b>cells&nbsp;</b>utilize <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>non-spontaneous</i></span> redox reactions using an <i>external</i> <u>emf source</u>&nbsp;to generate electrical activity."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Electrolytic</b></span>&nbsp;<b>cells&nbsp;</b>utilize <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>non-spontaneous</i></span> redox reactions using an <i>external</i> <u>emf source</u>&nbsp;to generate electrical activity.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Emf source drives <b>decomposition reactions</b>&nbsp;(breakdown of a compound into its constituent molecules).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""77a551346db25005363946de6f1f5bf1.png""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Electrolytic</b></span>&nbsp;<b>cells&nbsp;</b>utilize <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;non&#x2D;spontaneous&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[spont./non-spont.]</span> redox reactions using an <i>external</i> <u>emf source</u>&nbsp;to generate electrical activity."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Electrolytic</b></span>&nbsp;<b>cells&nbsp;</b>utilize <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>non-spontaneous</i></span> redox reactions using an <i>external</i> <u>emf source</u>&nbsp;to generate electrical activity.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Emf source drives <b>decomposition reactions</b>&nbsp;(breakdown of a compound into its constituent molecules).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""77a551346db25005363946de6f1f5bf1.png""><br></div></div>"
"In <b>electrolytic</b> cells, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Na&lt;sup&gt;&#x2B;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;ions from <u>molten NaCl</u> get <i>reduced</i> at the cathode into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Na&#x20;&#x28;l&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> after accepting electrons from the anode."	"In <b>electrolytic</b> cells, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Na<sup>+</sup></span>&nbsp;ions from <u>molten NaCl</u> get <i>reduced</i> at the cathode into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Na (l)</span> after accepting electrons from the anode.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Electrolytic cells are non-spontaneous because the decomposition of moltent NaCl would never occur on its own. Forcibly doing this (with a battery) generates electricity (due to the electron transfer).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""77a551346db25005363946de6f1f5bf1.png""><br></div></div>"
"In <b>electrolytic</b> cells, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Cl&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;ions from <u>molten NaCl</u> get <i>oxidized</i>&nbsp;at the anode into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Cl&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x28;g&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> after giving up its electrons to the cathode (generating a current)."	"In <b>electrolytic</b> cells, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Cl<sup>-</sup></span>&nbsp;ions from <u>molten NaCl</u> get <i>oxidized</i>&nbsp;at the anode into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Cl<sub>2</sub> (g)</span> after giving up its electrons to the cathode (generating a current).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Cl<sub>2</sub> (g) floats away after getting oxidized.</div><div>- Electrolytic cells are non-spontaneous because the decomposition of moltent NaCl would never occur on its own. Forcibly doing this (with a battery) generates electricity (due to the electron transfer).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""77a551346db25005363946de6f1f5bf1.png""><br></div></div>"
"In thermochemistry, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;standard&#x20;conditions&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[STP/standard cond.]</span>&nbsp;are:<div><br></div><div>Pressure = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 atm</span></div><div>Temperature = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">298 K (25°C)</span></div><div>Concentration = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 M</span></div>"	"In thermochemistry, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>standard conditions</b></span>&nbsp;are:<div><br></div><div>Pressure = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 atm</span></div><div>Temperature = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">298 K (25°C)</span></div><div>Concentration = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 M</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

These standard conditions are standardized because the system's final equilibrium will be different depending on what the pressure (P), temperature (T), or concentration of molecules (M) is.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;STP&#x20;conditions&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[STP/standard cond.]</span>&nbsp;used in the <u>ideal gas law</u>:<div><br></div><div>Pressure = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 atm</span></div><div>Temperature = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">273 K (0°C)</span></div>"	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>STP conditions</b></span>&nbsp;used in the <u>ideal gas law</u>:<div><br></div><div>Pressure = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 atm</span></div><div>Temperature = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">273 K (0°C)</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

These STP conditions are standardized because the system's final equilibrium will be different depending on what the pressure or temperature is.</div>"
"<div>In <b>electrolysis</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""moles&#x20;of&#x20;electrons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in the cell's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>current</u></span> is <i>equivalent </i>to the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""moles&#x20;of&#x20;electrons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> transferred in either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>half-reaction's redox</u></span> reaction.</div>"	"<div>In <b>electrolysis</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles of electrons</span> in the cell's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>current</u></span> is <i>equivalent </i>to the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles of electrons</span> transferred in either <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>half-reaction's redox</u></span> reaction.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>Faraday's first law of electrolysis</u></b>.</div><div>- More of a substance that plates onto the electrode after its redox reaction means there are more electrons flowing through the current.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1cf2dfb0e8f1ca265609fd39be8d96b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""294e30d032ab1837c1873567f00ce477.png""></div>"
"<div>In <b>electrolysis</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles of electrons</span> in the cell's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;current&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is <i>equivalent </i>to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles of electrons</span> transferred in either <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;half&#x2D;reaction&#x27;s&#x20;redox&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> reaction.</div>"	"<div>In <b>electrolysis</b>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles of electrons</span> in the cell's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>current</u></span> is <i>equivalent </i>to the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">moles of electrons</span> transferred in either <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>half-reaction's redox</u></span> reaction.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>Faraday's first law of electrolysis</u></b>.</div><div>- More of a substance that plates onto the electrode after its redox reaction means there are more electrons flowing through the current.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1cf2dfb0e8f1ca265609fd39be8d96b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""294e30d032ab1837c1873567f00ce477.png""></div>"
"One <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;faraday&#x20;&#x28;F&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[unit]</span> is the <i>amount </i>of <u>charge</u> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">one <u>mole of electrons</u></span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup> C/mol.e<sup>-</sup></span>."	"One <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>faraday (F)</b></span> is the <i>amount </i>of <u>charge</u> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">one <u>mole of electrons</u></span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup> C/mol.e<sup>-</sup></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>Faraday's constant (F)</u></b></div><div>- In <u>electrolysis</u>, this is stated as saying the amount of electrons a compound loses after its redox reaction is equivalent to the number of electrons in the current.</div><div>- Shows 'n' moles of electrons are needed to produce 1 mole of M (s).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1cf2dfb0e8f1ca265609fd39be8d96b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""294e30d032ab1837c1873567f00ce477.png""></div>"
"One <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>faraday (F)</b></span> is the <i>amount </i>of <u>charge</u> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">one <u>mole of electrons</u></span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x20;x&#x20;10&lt;sup&gt;5&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x20;C&#x2F;mol&#x2E;e&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span>."	"One <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>faraday (F)</b></span> is the <i>amount </i>of <u>charge</u> in <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">one <u>mole of electrons</u></span>: <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup> C/mol.e<sup>-</sup></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Example is a biological membrane.</div><div><br></div><img src=""23fb3666f2ae7339f6be5f0f40b5e2d1.png""></div>"
"<b>Rechargeable batteries</b> function as <i>both </i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""galvanic"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""electrolytic&#x20;cells"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<b>Rechargeable batteries</b> function as <i>both </i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">galvanic</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">electrolytic cells</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Galvanic</b>: discharging happens spontaneously (when the device is being used).<div>- <b>Electrolytic</b>: charging happens non-spontaneously (needs to get ""plugged in"" to reset the effects of the discharge)&nbsp;</div></div>"
"In a <b>lead-acid battery</b>, the <u>plates</u> when fully <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>charged</i></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Pb"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> (anode) &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""PbO&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> (cathode), with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub></span> in solution."	"In a <b>lead-acid battery</b>, the <u>plates</u> when fully <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>charged</i></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Pb</span> (anode) &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbO<sub>2</sub></span> (cathode), with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub></span> in solution.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> </b>drives a reaction that causes both anode &amp; cathode to turn into PbSO<sub>4</sub> (H<sub>2</sub>O is a byproduct, explaining why the solution becomes dilute).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7b80d3ca2b902afab980b6c885a0023fd2cb8e45.jpg""><br></div>"
"In a <b>lead-acid battery</b>, the <u>plates</u> when fully <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;charged&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[charged/discharged]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Pb</span> (anode) &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbO<sub>2</sub></span> (cathode), with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub></span> in solution."	"In a <b>lead-acid battery</b>, the <u>plates</u> when fully <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>charged</i></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Pb</span> (anode) &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbO<sub>2</sub></span> (cathode), with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub></span> in solution.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> </b>drives a reaction that causes both anode &amp; cathode to turn into PbSO<sub>4</sub> (H<sub>2</sub>O is a byproduct, explaining why the solution becomes dilute).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7b80d3ca2b902afab980b6c885a0023fd2cb8e45.jpg""><br></div>"

"In a <b>lead-acid battery</b>, the <u>plates</u> when fully <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>discharged</i></span> are coated with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbSO<sub>4</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbSO<sub>4</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>- H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> </b>drives a reaction that causes both anode &amp; cathode to turn into PbSO<sub>4</sub> (H<sub>2</sub>O is a byproduct, explaining why the solution becomes dilute).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-1c5256a82d39ea795067eb7cc0109a3b02759420.jpg""><br></div>"
"In a <b>lead-acid battery</b>, the <u>plates</u> when fully <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;discharged&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[charged/discharged]</span> are coated with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbSO<sub>4</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbSO<sub>4</sub></span>."	"In a <b>lead-acid battery</b>, the <u>plates</u> when fully <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>discharged</i></span> are coated with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbSO<sub>4</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">PbSO<sub>4</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>- H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> </b>drives a reaction that causes both anode &amp; cathode to turn into PbSO<sub>4</sub> (H<sub>2</sub>O is a byproduct, explaining why the solution becomes dilute).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-1c5256a82d39ea795067eb7cc0109a3b02759420.jpg""><br></div>"

"In a <b>lead-acid battery</b>, the process of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>discharging</i></span> represents a(n) <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>galvanic</u></span> cell since its reaction occurs <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">spontaneously</span>.

"<div>Elements with <i>high</i>&nbsp;<b>reduction potentials</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span>&nbsp;<u>electron affinities</u> - easily becoming <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""reduced"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ox./red.]</span>.</div>"	"<div>Elements with <i>high</i>&nbsp;<b>reduction potentials</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>high</i></span>&nbsp;<u>electron affinities</u> - easily becoming <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">reduced</span>.</div>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>High reduction potential: high electron affinity.</div><div>Low reduction potential: low electron affinity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ap7041.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Elements with <i>low</i>&nbsp;<b>reduction potentials</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[low/high]</span>&nbsp;<u>electron affinities</u> - becoming easily <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""oxidized"" data-ordinal=""1"">[ox./red.]</span>.</div>"	"<div>Elements with <i>low</i>&nbsp;<b>reduction potentials</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>low</i></span>&nbsp;<u>electron affinities</u> - becoming easily <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">oxidized</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>High reduction potential: high electron affinity.</div><div>Low reduction potential: low electron affinity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ap7041.jpg""></div>"
"In <b>galvanic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cathode"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span> naturally has a <i>high </i><u>reduction potential</u>."	"In <b>galvanic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cathode</span> naturally has a <i>high </i><u>reduction potential</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The cathode <u>spontaneously</u> becomes <b>reduced </b>through galvanic redox reactions.</div><div><br></div><div>High reduction potential: high electron affinity.</div><div>Low reduction potential: low electron affinity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ap7041.jpg""></div>"
"In <b>galvanic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""anode"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span> naturally has a <i>low&nbsp;</i><u>reduction potential</u>."	"In <b>galvanic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">anode</span> naturally has a <i>low&nbsp;</i><u>reduction potential</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The cathode <u>spontaneously</u> becomes <b>reduced </b>through galvanic redox reactions.</div><div><br></div><div>High reduction potential: high electron affinity.</div><div>Low reduction potential: low electron affinity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ap7041.jpg""></div>"
"In <b>electrolytic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""anode"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span> has a <i>high&nbsp;</i><u>reduction potential</u>."	"In <b>electrolytic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">anode</span> has a <i>high&nbsp;</i><u>reduction potential</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The&nbsp;<b>battery source</b>&nbsp;<u>forces</u>&nbsp;the species that normally has a low reduction potential to instead get reduced. This is why electrolytic cells are&nbsp;<u>non-spontaneous</u>.</div><div><br></div><div>High reduction potential: high electron affinity.</div><div>Low reduction potential: low electron affinity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ap7041.jpg""></div>"
"In <b>electrolytic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""cathode"" data-ordinal=""1"">[anode/cathode]</span> has a <i>low&nbsp;</i><u>reduction potential</u>."	"In <b>electrolytic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">cathode</span> has a <i>low&nbsp;</i><u>reduction potential</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The <b>battery source</b> <u>forces</u> the species that normally has a high reduction potential to instead get oxidized. This is why electrolytic cells are <u>non-spontaneous</u>.</div><div><br></div><div>High reduction potential: high electron affinity.</div><div>Low reduction potential: low electron affinity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ap7041.jpg""></div>"
"<div>To measure <b>standard reduction potentials (E°<sub>red</sub>)</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""hydrogen"" data-ordinal=""1"">[element]</span> is <i>arbitrarily</i> used as the <u>""zero"" point</u> that everything else is compared to.<br></div>"	"<div>To measure <b>standard reduction potentials (E°<sub>red</sub>)</b>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">hydrogen</span> is <i>arbitrarily</i> used as the <u>""zero"" point</u> that everything else is compared to.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>High reduction potential: high electron affinity.<br></div><div>Low reduction potential: low electron affinity.</div><div><br></div><img src=""ap7041.jpg""></div>"
"To calculate the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;standard&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;u&gt;emf&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&#x28;ε&#x20;or&#x20;E&#xB0;&lt;sub&gt;cell&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <i>produced </i>by an <u>electrochemical cell</u>, <i>subtract </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cathode's reduction potential</span> - <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">anode's reduction potential</span>."	"To calculate the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>standard&nbsp;<u>emf</u> (ε or E°<sub>cell</sub>)</b></span> <i>produced </i>by an <u>electrochemical cell</u>, <i>subtract </i>the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">cathode's reduction potential</span> - <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">anode's reduction potential</span>.

"The <b>standard free energy ΔG°</b> in an <u>electrochemical cell</u> will<i> always</i> have the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;opposite&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[same/opposite]</span> sign from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">generated emf (E°<sub>cell</sub>)</span>." <div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Calculates the emf of an electrochemical cell when its ion concentrations deviate from standard conditions.</div></div>"
"<div>Mathematically, <b>log </b>&amp; <b>ln</b> will be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;positive&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[+/-]</span> values if the number is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greater </i>than 1</span>.</div>"	"<div>Mathematically, <b>log </b>&amp; <b>ln</b> will be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>positive</u></span> values if the number is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>greater </i>than 1</span>.</div>

</div>"

</div>"

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Graham&#x27;s&#x20;law&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[law]</span> says that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>larger</u> gas particles diffuse/effuse at <i>slower </i>rates</span>&nbsp;than <u>smaller</u> gas particles."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Graham's law</b></span> says that <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>larger</u> gas particles diffuse/effuse at <i>slower </i>rates</span>&nbsp;than <u>smaller</u> gas particles.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>R</b>&nbsp;=&nbsp;<u>rates</u>&nbsp;of diffusion/effusion (gas 1 &amp; gas 2);&nbsp;<b>M</b>&nbsp;=&nbsp;<u>molar mass</u>&nbsp;of each gas.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""9160ba5b4e25c9234897de077a10a373.png""><div><div><br></div><div><img src=""3625c272f92029618e664e1dc32201b7.png""></div></div></div>"

"A carbon <u>double bond</u> has a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>higher</i></span> <b>bond order</b> than a carbon <u>single bond</u>.

"Compounds with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>ionic</b></span><b>&nbsp;bonds</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lower/higher]</span> <u>melting points</u> than ones with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>hydrogen bonds</b></span>." "When&nbsp;<b>freezing point depression</b>&nbsp;occurs in a compound, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""melting&#x20;point&#x20;depression"" data-ordinal=""1"">[colligative prop.]</span> <i>also</i> occurs."	"When&nbsp;<b>freezing point depression</b>&nbsp;occurs in a compound, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">melting point depression</span> <i>also</i> occurs.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Melting point depression means you don't need as much heat as before to disrupt the lattice structure, since solutes already disrupt the lattice. This also explains freezing point depression.</div><div>- Δ<b>T<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= freezing point depression;&nbsp;<b>i&nbsp;</b>= Van't hoff factor (<u>number</u>&nbsp;of particles the dissolved compound splits into...i=2 for NaCl);&nbsp;<b>K<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= constant (given);&nbsp;<b>m</b>&nbsp;= molarity</div><div><br></div><img src=""396da012cca358efc19f5cea11f91c6e.png""></div>"
"In terms of colligative properties, <u>acids</u><b> </b>have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;larger&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[smaller/larger]</span> <b>i</b>'s than insoluble compounds in solution."	"In terms of colligative properties, <u>acids</u><b> </b>have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> <b>i</b>'s than insoluble compounds in solution.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because acids dissociate into multiple compounds in solution, meaning they split into more molecules.</div><div><br></div><div>Even lower temperatures are needed to freeze, in this case.</div><div>- Δ<b>T<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= freezing point depression;&nbsp;<b>i&nbsp;</b>= Van't hoff factor (<u>number</u>&nbsp;of particles the dissolved compound splits into...i=2 for NaCl);&nbsp;<b>K<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= constant (given);&nbsp;<b>m</b>&nbsp;= molarity</div><div><br></div><img src=""396da012cca358efc19f5cea11f91c6e.png""></div>"
"1 <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">atm</span>&nbsp;= <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""760"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> mmHg = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""760"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> Torr = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup></span> Pa."	"1 <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">atm</span>&nbsp;= <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">760</span> mmHg = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">760</span> Torr = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup></span> Pa.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Torr</b> is just another way to say ""mmHg.""</div>"
"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""atm"" data-ordinal=""2"">[unit]</span>&nbsp;= <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">760</span> mmHg = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">760</span> Torr = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup></span> Pa."	"1 <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">atm</span>&nbsp;= <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">760</span> mmHg = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">760</span> Torr = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup></span> Pa.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>Torr</b> is just another way to say ""mmHg.""</div>"
"<div>In the equation for <u>freezing point depression</u>, <b>m</b> represents <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""molality&#x20;&#x28;mol&#x2F;kg&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""396da012cca358efc19f5cea11f91c6e.png"">"	"<div>In the equation for <u>freezing point depression</u>, <b>m</b> represents <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">molality (mol/kg)</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""396da012cca358efc19f5cea11f91c6e.png"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<u>Same</u>&nbsp;equation as&nbsp;<b>boiling point elevation</b>.</div><div>- Δ<b>T<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= freezing point depression;&nbsp;<b>i&nbsp;</b>= Van't hoff factor (<u>number</u>&nbsp;of particles the dissolved compound splits into...i=2 for NaCl);&nbsp;<b>K<sub>f</sub></b>&nbsp;= constant (given);&nbsp;<b>m</b>&nbsp;= molality (mol/kg).</div></div>"
"<div>Gases with a <b>PV/RT</b> ratio <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;below&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[above/below]</span><i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>&nbsp;exhibit <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>intermolecular attractions</u></span> - causing it to behave more real.</div>"	"<div>Gases with a <b>PV/RT</b> ratio <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>below</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;exhibit <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>intermolecular attractions</u></span> - causing it to behave more real.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>Gases with a <b>PV/RT</b> ratio <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>below</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;exhibit <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;intermolecular&#x20;attractions&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - causing it to behave more real.</div>"	"<div>Gases with a <b>PV/RT</b> ratio <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>below</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;exhibit <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>intermolecular attractions</u></span> - causing it to behave more real.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>Gases with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>PV/RT</b> ratio</span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;above&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[above/below]</span><i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span>&nbsp;exhibit <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>intermolecular repulsions</u></span> - causing it to behave more real.</div>"	"<div>Gases with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>PV/RT</b> ratio</span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>above</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;exhibit <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>intermolecular repulsions</u></span> - causing it to behave more real.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>Gases with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>PV/RT</b> ratio</span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>above</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;exhibit <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;intermolecular&#x20;repulsions&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> - causing it to behave more real.</div>"	"<div>Gases with a <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>PV/RT</b> ratio</span>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>above</i></span><i>&nbsp;</i><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span>&nbsp;exhibit <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>intermolecular repulsions</u></span> - causing it to behave more real.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

</div>"
"<div>Gases with a <b>PV/RT</b> ratio <i>equal to</i> 1 behave as <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ideal"" data-ordinal=""1"">[real/ideal]</span> gases.</div>"	"<div>Gases with a <b>PV/RT</b> ratio <i>equal to</i> 1 behave as <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ideal</span> gases.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Ideal gases <u>do not</u> exhibit intermolecular attractions.&nbsp;<div><br></div><div>- <b>PV/RT</b> ratio <u>below 1</u>: indicates intermolecular attractions (real gas).</div><div>- <b>PV/RT</b> ratio <u>above 1</u>: indicates intermolecular repulsions (real gas).</div></div>"
"<b>Fusion</b> is the<i> same thing</i> as <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;melting&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - referring to the transition from <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""solid"" data-ordinal=""1"">[phase]</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""liquid"" data-ordinal=""1"">[phase]</span>."	"<b>Fusion</b> is the<i> same thing</i> as <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>melting</u></span> - referring to the transition from <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">solid</span> to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">liquid</span>.

"<div>On a <u>titration curve</u>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>half-equivalence point</b></span> is the point at which <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pH"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pK&lt;sub&gt;a&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x5B;HA&#x5D;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x5B;A&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x5D;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>On a <u>titration curve</u>, the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>half-equivalence point</b></span> is the point at which <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">pH</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">pK<sub>a</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">[HA]</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">[A<sup>-</sup>]</span>.<br></div>

This is shown with the <b>Henderson-Hasselbalch</b> equation below.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""main-qimg-cf2f53b7dbf9c04434a2706f68221454.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""af26ca2388f6bd7ec4bb22326645e6f4.png""><br></div></div>"
"<div>On a <u>titration curve</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;half&#x2D;equivalence&#x20;point&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is the point at which <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">pH</span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">pK<sub>a</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">[HA]</span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">[A<sup>-</sup>]</span>.<br></div>"	"<div>On a <u>titration curve</u>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>half-equivalence point</b></span> is the point at which <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">pH</span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">pK<sub>a</sub></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">[HA]</span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">[A<sup>-</sup>]</span>.<br></div>

<div>When [A<sup>-</sup>] = [HA], pH = pK<sub>a</sub>. This is shown with the <b>Henderson-Hasselbalch</b> equation below.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""main-qimg-cf2f53b7dbf9c04434a2706f68221454.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""af26ca2388f6bd7ec4bb22326645e6f4.png""><br></div></div>"
"The <b>water dissociation constant (K<sub>w</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1&#x20;x&#x20;10&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;14&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> at 25°C."	"The <b>water dissociation constant (K<sub>w</sub>)</b> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1 x 10<sup>-14</sup></span> at 25°C.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is the constant that represents water's tendency to ionize into H<sub>3</sub>O<sup>+</sup> &amp; OH<sup>-</sup></div><div>- This constant (K<sub>w</sub>) explains why the pH scale goes up to 14.</div><div><br></div><img src=""f2420afca3398f6e669fe14321aac785.png""></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;water&#x20;dissociation&#x20;constant&#x20;&#x28;K&lt;sub&gt;w&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[constant]</span> represents water's tendency to <i>ionize</i> into H<sub>3</sub>O<sup>+</sup> and OH<sup>-</sup> in solution at 25°C.</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>water dissociation constant (K<sub>w</sub>)</b></span> represents water's tendency to <i>ionize</i> into H<sub>3</sub>O<sup>+</sup> and OH<sup>-</sup> in solution at 25°C.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>1 x 10<sup>-14</sup></b>.</div><div>- This constant (K<sub>w</sub>) explains why the pH scale goes up to 14.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-276f19d597c0560532ec4533e879a8d97d783781.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""f2420afca3398f6e669fe14321aac785.png""></div>"
"The <b>water dissociation constant (K<sub>w</sub>)</b><i> only</i> changes in magnitude once <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""temperature&#x20;&#x28;K&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[property]</span> is changed."	"The <b>water dissociation constant (K<sub>w</sub>)</b><i> only</i> changes in magnitude once <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">temperature (K)</span> is changed.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>1 x 10<sup>-14</sup></b></div><div>- The only property this is dependent on is temperature.</div><div><br></div><img src=""f2420afca3398f6e669fe14321aac785.png""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pH"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">-log[H<sup>+</sup>]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">pH</span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">-log[H<sup>+</sup>]</span>.
<hr>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pOH"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">-log[OH<sup>-</sup>]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">pOH</span> = <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">-log[OH<sup>-</sup>]</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">pOH</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2D;log&#x5B;OH&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;&#x5D;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">pOH</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">-log[OH<sup>-</sup>]</span>.

"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">pH</span> = <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">-log[H<sup>+</sup>]</span>.
