"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;dilution&#x20;factor&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> by which a compound is diluted:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-ecdabf970ccda2f194d0df8bc91af1a58c6374bb.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>dilution factor</b></span> by which a compound is diluted:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-ecdabf970ccda2f194d0df8bc91af1a58c6374bb.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>DF</b> = dilution factor; <b>V<sub>T1/2</sub></b> = volume transferred in step 1/2; <b>V<sub>F1/2</sub></b> = final volume after step 1/2.</div>"
"<div>In the equation for the dilution factor, <b>V<sub>T1/2</sub></b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""initial&#x20;volume&#x20;&lt;i&gt;transferred&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;in&#x20;step&#x20;1&#x2F;2"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ecdabf970ccda2f194d0df8bc91af1a58c6374bb.jpg"">"	"<div>In the equation for the dilution factor, <b>V<sub>T1/2</sub></b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">initial volume <i>transferred</i> in step 1/2</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ecdabf970ccda2f194d0df8bc91af1a58c6374bb.jpg"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>DF</b> = dilution factor; <b>V<sub>T1/2</sub></b> = volume transferred in step 1/2; <b>V<sub>F1/2</sub></b> = final volume after step 1/2.</div>"
"<div>In the equation for the dilution factor, <b>V<sub>F1/2</sub></b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""final&#x20;volume&#x20;&lt;i&gt;after&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;step&#x20;1&#x2F;2"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ecdabf970ccda2f194d0df8bc91af1a58c6374bb.jpg"">"	"<div>In the equation for the dilution factor, <b>V<sub>F1/2</sub></b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">final volume <i>after</i>&nbsp;step 1/2</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ecdabf970ccda2f194d0df8bc91af1a58c6374bb.jpg"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>DF</b> = dilution factor (expressed as a ratio); <b>V<sub>T1/2</sub></b> = volume transferred in step 1/2; <b>V<sub>F1/2</sub></b> = final volume after step 1/2.</div>"
"<div>In the equation for the dilution factor, <b>DF</b>&nbsp;is the dilution factor, where the <u>dilution ratio</u> is expressed as a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""fraction&#x20;&#x28;1&#x3A;20&#x20;dilution&#x20;→&#x20;1&#x2F;20&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ecdabf970ccda2f194d0df8bc91af1a58c6374bb.jpg"">"	"<div>In the equation for the dilution factor, <b>DF</b>&nbsp;is the dilution factor, where the <u>dilution ratio</u> is expressed as a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">fraction (1:20 dilution → 1/20)</span>.<br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ecdabf970ccda2f194d0df8bc91af1a58c6374bb.jpg"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>DF</b> = dilution factor; <b>V<sub>T1/2</sub></b> = volume transferred in step 1/2; <b>V<sub>F1/2</sub></b> = final volume after step 1/2.<div>- If the dilution factor is 1:20, DF is 1/20.</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Chlorite</b></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""ClO&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;sup&gt;&#x2D;&lt;&#x2F;sup&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>, with a formal charge of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x2D;1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Chlorite</b></span> is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">ClO<sub>2</sub><sup>-</sup></span>, with a formal charge of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">-1</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- ClO<sub>3</sub><sup>-</sup> → Chlorate<div>- ClO<sub>2</sub><sup>-</sup> → Chlorite</div><div>- ClO<sup>-</sup> → Hypochlorite</div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Chlorite&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">ClO<sub>2</sub><sup>-</sup></span>, with a formal charge of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">-1</span>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>Chlorite</b></span> is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">ClO<sub>2</sub><sup>-</sup></span>, with a formal charge of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">-1</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- ClO<sub>3</sub><sup>-</sup> → Chlorate<div>- ClO<sub>2</sub><sup>-</sup> → Chlorite</div><div>- ClO<sup>-</sup> → Hypochlorite</div></div>"

"In a coordinate complex, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;coordination&#x20;number&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u><i>number</i> of coordinate bonds</u></span> between the central cation &amp; the ligands." "Steps to calculating the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;limiting&#x20;reagent&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> in a reaction:<div><br></div><div>1. Calculate the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>moles of <i>each</i> reactant</u></span>.</div><div>2. Use the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mole ratio</u></span> <i>between</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>reactant</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>product</u></span> to determine the moles of product that could be formed.</div><div>3. Choose the reactant that yields the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>least</i> amount of product</span>.</div>"	"Steps to calculating the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>limiting reagent</b></span> in a reaction:<div><br></div><div>1. Calculate the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>moles of <i>each</i> reactant</u></span>.</div><div>2. Use the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>mole ratio</u></span> <i>between</i> the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>reactant</u></span> &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>product</u></span> to determine the moles of product that could be formed.</div><div>3. "Bonds between two elements in the <i>same</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">row (left → right)</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>easier</i></span> to <u>break apart</u> as you move <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><i>down</i></span> the periodic table.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: C-C bond (row 2) is easier to break apart than an H-H bond (row 1).</div><div>- These bonds are easier to break apart because the atomic radius of each element increases (higher 'n') - making them longer in length (held less tightly).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3771bf86b5f1da22f4c857948fa546e2d309a131.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3bd3a5bd29c561536ae6e3f5672c4fbb377cf3dd.jpg""><br></div></div>"

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- When bonds are formed between two elements in the same row (left → right), they become easier to break apart as you move down the periodic table. This is because atomic radii increases - lengthening the bond &amp; making it easier to break apart.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3771bf86b5f1da22f4c857948fa546e2d309a131.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3bd3a5bd29c561536ae6e3f5672c4fbb377cf3dd.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"H-H bonds are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;harder&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[easier/harder]</span> to <u>break apart</u> than C-C bonds."	"H-H bonds are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>harder</i></span> to <u>break apart</u> than C-C bonds.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- When bonds are formed between two elements in the same row (left → right), they become easier to break apart as you move down the periodic table. This is because atomic radii increases - lengthening the bond &amp; making it easier to break apart.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3771bf86b5f1da22f4c857948fa546e2d309a131.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3bd3a5bd29c561536ae6e3f5672c4fbb377cf3dd.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Bonds formed between <u>two atoms</u> with <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;small&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[small/large]</span> <b>atomic radii</b> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>stronger</i></span> due to having more electron overlapping."	"Bonds formed between <u>two atoms</u> with <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>small</i></span> <b>atomic radii</b> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>stronger</i></span> due to having more electron overlapping.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- When bonds are formed between two elements in the same row (left → right), they become easier to break apart as you move down the periodic table. This is because atomic radii increases - lengthening the bond &amp; making it easier to break apart.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3771bf86b5f1da22f4c857948fa546e2d309a131.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3bd3a5bd29c561536ae6e3f5672c4fbb377cf3dd.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"Bonds formed between <u>two atoms</u> with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>small</i></span> <b>atomic radii</b> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>stronger</i></span> due to having more electron overlapping.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- When bonds are formed between two elements in the same row (left → right), they become easier to break apart as you move down the periodic table. This is because atomic radii increases - lengthening the bond &amp; making it easier to break apart.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-3771bf86b5f1da22f4c857948fa546e2d309a131.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-3bd3a5bd29c561536ae6e3f5672c4fbb377cf3dd.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Symmetrical</u></span> molecules <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;cannot&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> have <b>net dipole moments</b>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Symmetrical</u></span> molecules <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> have <b>net dipole moments</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Only asymmetrical molecules have net dipole moments. That's why only the middle compound shows a net dipole moment (it's asymmetrical).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-8083851c41e1464181b929e04109c313a018fadf.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<u>Asymmetrical</u> molecules <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;can&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> have <b>net dipole moments</b>."	"<u>Asymmetrical</u> molecules <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>can</i></span> have <b>net dipole moments</b>.
<hr>

"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;Symmetrical&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[symm./asymm.]</span> molecules <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> have <b>net dipole moments</b>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>Symmetrical</u></span> molecules <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> have <b>net dipole moments</b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Only asymmetrical molecules have net dipole moments. That's why only the middle compound shows a net dipole moment (it's asymmetrical).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-8083851c41e1464181b929e04109c313a018fadf.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"<b>Dipole-dipole forces</b> can <i>only</i> form between <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>polar</u></span> molecules.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""paste-7f5a5787bba6fed8f15843ce088f1384041b72b1.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;London&#x20;dispersion&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> <b>forces</b> are <i>also</i> called <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>induced</i> dipole</span> forces.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>London dispersion</b></span> <b>forces</b> are <i>also</i> called <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>induced</i> dipole</span> forces.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Induced dipole forces <u>are not</u> considered dipole-dipole forces. Think of the ""electron bucket"" analogy.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f5a5787bba6fed8f15843ce088f1384041b72b1.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>London dispersion</b></span> <b>forces</b> are <i>also</i> called <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;induced&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;dipole"" data-ordinal=""2"">[dipole]</span> forces.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>London dispersion</b></span> <b>forces</b> are <i>also</i> called <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>induced</i> dipole</span> forces.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Induced dipole forces <u>are not</u> considered dipole-dipole forces. Think of the ""electron bucket"" analogy.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f5a5787bba6fed8f15843ce088f1384041b72b1.jpg""></div>"
"<div><b>Induced dipole forces</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;are&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[are/are not]</span> considered <u>dipole-dipole forces</u>.<br></div>"	"<div><b>Induced dipole forces</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>are not</i></span> considered <u>dipole-dipole forces</u>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Think of the ""electron bucket"" analogy.</div><div>- Dipole-dipole forces form <u>net dipoles</u> pointing in a single direction.</div><div>- Induced dipole forces are random and <u>do not</u> form a single net dipole force.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f5a5787bba6fed8f15843ce088f1384041b72b1.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Dipole&#x2D;dipole"" data-ordinal=""1"">[dipole]</span> forces result in strong <u>net dipole moments</u> pointing in a <i>single</i> direction, whereas <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""induced&#x20;dipole"" data-ordinal=""1"">[dipole]</span> forces are totally random.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Dipole-dipole</span> forces result in strong <u>net dipole moments</u> pointing in a <i>single</i> direction, whereas <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">induced dipole</span> forces are totally random.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Think of the ""electron bucket"" analogy.</div><div>- Dipole-dipole forces form <u>net dipoles</u> pointing in a single direction.</div><div>- Induced dipole forces are random and <u>do not</u> form a single net dipole force.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-7f5a5787bba6fed8f15843ce088f1384041b72b1.jpg""></div>"
"Before drawing a <b>Lewis structure</b>, <i>first</i> count the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;total&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;number&#x20;of&#x20;valence&#x20;electrons"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"Before drawing a <b>Lewis structure</b>, <i>first</i> count the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>total</i> number of valence electrons</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Knowing that SO<sub>2</sub> contains a total of 18 electrons explains why its Lewis structure is drawn the way it is.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-60ad4f08ca2143c9ab4ac599df39d78407579b6d.jpg""></div>"
"On a titration curve, the <u>buffer region</u> is at the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""half&#x2D;equivalence&#x20;point"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"On a titration curve, the <u>buffer region</u> is at the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">half-equivalence point</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The <b>half-equivalence point</b> is where...</div><div>- pH = pKa</div><div>- [HA] = [A<sup>-</sup>]</div><div><br></div><img src=""main-qimg-cf2f53b7dbf9c04434a2706f68221454.png""></div>"
"In acid-base titrations, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;indicators&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weak</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">acids/bases</span>&nbsp;that <u>change color</u> depending on whether they're in their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">protonated or deprotonated form</span>."	"In acid-base titrations, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>indicators</b></span> are <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weak</i></span> <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">acids/bases</span>&nbsp;that <u>change color</u> depending on whether they're in their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">protonated or deprotonated form</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-642e02422cf9ec84c0b43b9c95c49d4145f72e75.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e22cddc3324604a8785b3f6c8abfc03fa83ba97a.jpg""></div>"
"In acid-base titrations, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>indicators</b></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;weak&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[weak/strong]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""acids&#x2F;bases"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>&nbsp;that <u>change color</u> depending on whether they're in their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">protonated or deprotonated form</span>."	"In acid-base titrations, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>indicators</b></span> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>weak</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">acids/bases</span>&nbsp;that <u>change color</u> depending on whether they're in their <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">protonated or deprotonated form</span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><img src=""paste-642e02422cf9ec84c0b43b9c95c49d4145f72e75.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e22cddc3324604a8785b3f6c8abfc03fa83ba97a.jpg""></div>"
"In acid-base titrations, the <i>best</i> <b>pH</b>&nbsp;<b>indicator</b> to use should have a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pK&lt;sub&gt;a&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;within&#x20;&#xB1;1"" data-ordinal=""2"">[#]</span> from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;equivalence&#x20;point&#x27;s&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;pH"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>." <hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>The equivalence point is the area of focus because it shows us when the solution takes a dramatic change in pH.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a91dc8b3cbdf8b7e6bbef5a89fbbbc4d769eebc9.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In acid-base titrations, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;endpoint&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is reached when the pH indicator reaches its full color, which happens when the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">pK<sub>a(indicator)</sub> = pH<sub>equivalence point</sub></span>."	"In acid-base titrations, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>endpoint</b></span> is reached when the pH indicator reaches its full color, which happens when the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">pK<sub>a(indicator)</sub> = pH<sub>equivalence point</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Indicators should have pK<sub>a</sub> values close to the <b>equivalence point's</b> pH.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-a91dc8b3cbdf8b7e6bbef5a89fbbbc4d769eebc9.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In acid-base titrations, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;titrant&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the solution you're <i>adding</i> drop-wise to the mixture."	"In acid-base titrations, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>titrant</b></span> is the solution you're <i>adding</i> drop-wise to the mixture.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

"In acid-base titrations, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;titrand&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> is the mixture whose pH you're analyzing."	"In acid-base titrations, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>titrand</b></span> is the mixture whose pH you're analyzing.

"
"A <b>neutral molecule</b> and its corresponding <b>anion/cation</b> are considered <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;different&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[same/different]</span> <u>compound(s)</u>, with chemically <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;distinct&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[identical/distinct]</span> properties."	"A <b>neutral molecule</b> and its corresponding <b>anion/cation</b> are considered <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>different</i></span> <u>compound(s)</u>, with chemically <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>distinct</i></span> properties.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Considered different because they have a different number of electrons and bonding pattern.</div><div>- Have different chemical and physical properties.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-4b3f15a98ebd180e971c5ccec31d2e95ae3683a6.jpg""></div>"
"One <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>faraday (F)</b></span> is the <i>amount </i>of <u>charge</u> in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""one&#x20;&lt;u&gt;mole&#x20;of&#x20;electrons&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup> C/mol.e<sup>-</sup></span>."	"One <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>faraday (F)</b></span> is the <i>amount </i>of <u>charge</u> in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">one <u>mole of electrons</u></span>: <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1 x 10<sup>5</sup> C/mol.e<sup>-</sup></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b><u>Faraday's constant (F)</u></b></div><div>- In <u>electrolysis</u>, this is stated as saying the amount of electrons a compound loses after its redox reaction is equivalent to the number of electrons in the current.</div><div>- Shows 'n' moles of electrons are needed to produce 1 mole of M (s).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""d1cf2dfb0e8f1ca265609fd39be8d96b.png""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""294e30d032ab1837c1873567f00ce477.png""></div>"
"<div>In&nbsp;<b>galvanic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;cathode&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[anode/cathode]</span>&nbsp;uses a Copper (Cu) bar with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">CuSO<sub>4</sub></span> ions in solution, which get&nbsp;<i>reduced </i>into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Cu (s)</span> after&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;receiving&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;the&#x20;anode&#x27;s&#x20;electrons"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>In&nbsp;<b>galvanic cells</b>, the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>cathode</u></span>&nbsp;uses a Copper (Cu) bar with <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">CuSO<sub>4</sub></span> ions in solution, which get&nbsp;<i>reduced </i>into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">Cu (s)</span> after&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>receiving</i>&nbsp;the anode's electrons</span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Electrons travel through the <u>copper wire</u> to reach the cathode (Cu (s)) from the anode (Zn (s)).</div><div>- Reduction from Cu<sup>2+</sup> → Cu (s) causes copper to solidify on the cathode itself.</div><div><br></div><div><div><img src=""098cf7ba35a1957a943cf3c8c111ff96.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""8ebfa5a776631ba0a62e93d2dcfcd66a.png""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""9f5660936ee87c6cdbdff58c42d55525.png""></div></div></div>"

"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pH</b></span>&nbsp;of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>equivalence point</u></span> on a titration curve of a polyprotic acid can be calculated by <i>averaging</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;pK&lt;sub&gt;a&#x28;buffer1&#x29;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x2B;&#x20;pK&lt;sub&gt;a&#x28;buffer2&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>&nbsp;/ <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>."	"The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>pH</b></span>&nbsp;of the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>equivalence point</u></span> on a titration curve of a polyprotic acid can be calculated by <i>averaging</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">(pK<sub>a(buffer1)&nbsp;</sub>+ pK<sub>a(buffer2)</sub>)</span>&nbsp;/ <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span>.

<div>- This is just like a regular galvanic cell, except it happens to have its reactants/products continually replenished/removed.</div><div>- Just like all other galvanic cells, it's <u>spontaneous</u> (+ε).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-0d8e73bc6e3d07fdd1252165f466f9d3436d7d98.jpg""></div>"

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- This is just like a regular galvanic cell, except it happens to have its reactants/products continually replenished/removed.</div><div>- Just like all other galvanic cells, it's <u>spontaneous</u> (+ε).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-0d8e73bc6e3d07fdd1252165f466f9d3436d7d98.jpg""></div>"
"To <u style="""">charge</u> a battery, the applied <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>potential difference (ΔV)</b></span> must be <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;greater&#x20;than&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[less/greater/equal]</span> the potential (V) the cell spontaneously produces in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>force the <i>reverse</i> reaction</u></span>."	"To <u style="""">charge</u> a battery, the applied <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>potential difference (ΔV)</b></span> must be <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>greater than</i></span> the potential (V) the cell spontaneously produces in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>force the <i>reverse</i> reaction</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The potential supplied by the external battery source is <u>positive</u>.</div><div>- Needs to be greater than the potential of the original spontaneous reaction in order to overcome some (inherent) slight internal resistance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-afeb6b8d7f38018e2d70bd99ce7b04403c5b995f.jpg""></div>"
"To <u style="""">charge</u> a battery, the applied <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>potential difference (ΔV)</b></span> must be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>greater than</i></span> the potential (V) the cell spontaneously produces in order to <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;force&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;reverse&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;reaction&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"To <u style="""">charge</u> a battery, the applied <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>potential difference (ΔV)</b></span> must be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>greater than</i></span> the potential (V) the cell spontaneously produces in order to <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>force the <i>reverse</i> reaction</u></span>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The potential supplied by the external battery source is <u>positive</u>.</div><div>- Needs to be greater than the potential of the original spontaneous reaction in order to overcome some (inherent) slight internal resistance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-afeb6b8d7f38018e2d70bd99ce7b04403c5b995f.jpg""></div>"

"To <u style="""">charge</u> a battery, the applied <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><b>potential difference (ΔV)</b></span> must be <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>greater than</i></span> the potential (V) the cell spontaneously produces in order to <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>force the <i>reverse</i> reaction</u></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The potential supplied by the external battery source is <u>positive</u>.</div><div>- Needs to be greater than the potential of the original spontaneous reaction in order to overcome some (inherent) slight internal resistance.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-afeb6b8d7f38018e2d70bd99ce7b04403c5b995f.jpg""></div>"
"When a <u>lead-acid battery</u> is being <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""discharged"" data-ordinal=""1"">[charged/discharged]</span>, the solution <i>gradually</i> forms into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O + H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub></span>."	"When a <u>lead-acid battery</u> is being <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">discharged</span>, the solution <i>gradually</i> forms into <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O + H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The plates when fully discharged are PbSO<sub>4</sub> &amp; PbSO<sub>4</sub>, with H<sub>2</sub>O and H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> in solution.</div><div>- H<sub>2</sub>O arises from a double-displacement reaction between PbO<sub>2</sub> and H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-1c5256a82d39ea795067eb7cc0109a3b02759420.jpg""></div>"
"When a <u>lead-acid battery</u> is being <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">discharged</span>, the solution <i>gradually</i> forms into <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""H&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;O&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;H&lt;sub&gt;2&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;SO&lt;sub&gt;4&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>."	"When a <u>lead-acid battery</u> is being <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1"">discharged</span>, the solution <i>gradually</i> forms into <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">H<sub>2</sub>O + H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub></span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The plates when fully discharged are PbSO<sub>4</sub> &amp; PbSO<sub>4</sub>, with H<sub>2</sub>O and H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> in solution.</div><div>- H<sub>2</sub>O arises from a double-displacement reaction between PbO<sub>2</sub> and H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-1c5256a82d39ea795067eb7cc0109a3b02759420.jpg""></div>"
"<div>A battery's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>energy density</b></span> represents the amount of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;power&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&#x28;W&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> it can produce <i>per</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""kg&#x20;of&#x20;its&#x20;&lt;u&gt;weight&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span>.</div>"	"<div>A battery's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>energy density</b></span> represents the amount of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>power&nbsp;(W)</u></span> it can produce <i>per</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">kg of its <u>weight</u></span>.</div>

<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<b>Energy</b> (Watts); <b>mass</b> (kg).</div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;electromotive&#x20;force&#x20;&#x28;E&#xB0;&lt;sub&gt;cell&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a battery is the amount of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>work</u></span> <i>per</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">unit of <u>charge</u></span> a battery can perform from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">current (I)</span> it generates by its potential difference (ΔV).</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>electromotive force (E°<sub>cell</sub>)</b></span> of a battery is the amount of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>work</u></span> <i>per</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">unit of <u>charge</u></span> a battery can perform from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">current (I)</span> it generates by its potential difference (ΔV).</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- When a battery is measured in terms of its E°<sub>cell</sub>, it's saying ""this battery can do ___ amount of work due to the charges it produces.""<div>- Batteries produce electrical currents (I) through redox reactions (like lead-acid batteries).</div><div>- As soon as charge begins to flow, the emf becomes known as the voltage.</div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>electromotive force (E°<sub>cell</sub>)</b></span> of a battery is the amount of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;work&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> <i>per</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""unit&#x20;of&#x20;&lt;u&gt;charge&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> a battery can perform from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">current (I)</span> it generates by its potential difference (ΔV).</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>electromotive force (E°<sub>cell</sub>)</b></span> of a battery is the amount of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>work</u></span> <i>per</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">unit of <u>charge</u></span> a battery can perform from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">current (I)</span> it generates by its potential difference (ΔV).</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- When a battery is measured in terms of its E°<sub>cell</sub>, it's saying ""this battery can do ___ amount of work due to the charges it produces.""<div>- Batteries produce electrical currents (I) through redox reactions (like lead-acid batteries).</div><div>- As soon as charge begins to flow, the emf becomes known as the voltage.</div></div>"
"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>electromotive force (E°<sub>cell</sub>)</b></span> of a battery is the amount of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>work</u></span> <i>per</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">unit of <u>charge</u></span> a battery can perform from the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""current&#x20;&#x28;I&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> it generates by its potential difference (ΔV).</div>"	"<div>The <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>electromotive force (E°<sub>cell</sub>)</b></span> of a battery is the amount of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>work</u></span> <i>per</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">unit of <u>charge</u></span> a battery can perform from the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">current (I)</span> it generates by its potential difference (ΔV).</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- When a battery is measured in terms of its E°<sub>cell</sub>, it's saying ""this battery can do ___ amount of work due to the charges it produces.""<div>- Batteries produce electrical currents (I) through redox reactions (like lead-acid batteries).</div><div>- As soon as charge begins to flow, the emf becomes known as the voltage.</div></div>"
"<b>Metal</b> <b>corrosion</b> happens the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;fastest&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[slowest/fastest]</span> when they're in the presence of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>highly</i></span> <u>soluble salts</u>."	"<b>Metal</b> <b>corrosion</b> happens the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>fastest</i></span> when they're in the presence of <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>highly</i></span> <u>soluble salts</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because highly soluble salts easily dissociate into anions - which the metals bind to (corrosion).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-19c25ce20fcb6971100e4443c67828a4d68d7bd9.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7ed596464b512a6d463521fdca353e5a406c1c5a.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<b>Metal</b> <b>corrosion</b> happens the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>fastest</i></span> when they're in the presence of <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;highly&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[slightly/highly]</span> <u>soluble salts</u>."	"<b>Metal</b> <b>corrosion</b> happens the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>fastest</i></span> when they're in the presence of <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>highly</i></span> <u>soluble salts</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because highly soluble salts easily dissociate into anions - which the metals bind to (corrosion).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-19c25ce20fcb6971100e4443c67828a4d68d7bd9.jpg""><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7ed596464b512a6d463521fdca353e5a406c1c5a.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"Highly <u>soluble salts</u> <i>accelerate</i> the rates of <b>metal corrosion</b> because they <i>easily</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""dissociate&#x20;into&#x20;ions"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - allowing the metal to bind &amp; form metal oxides."	"Highly <u>soluble salts</u> <i>accelerate</i> the rates of <b>metal corrosion</b> because they <i>easily</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">dissociate into ions</span> - allowing the metal to bind &amp; form metal oxides.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Corrosion typically happens when metals <u>combine</u> with other ions to form <b>metal oxides</b>.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-19c25ce20fcb6971100e4443c67828a4d68d7bd9.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-7ed596464b512a6d463521fdca353e5a406c1c5a.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"The <u>dissolution reaction</u> of a compound <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;cannot&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> be entirely stopped due to the <b>common ion effect</b> - <i>only</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""slowed"" data-ordinal=""1"">[slowed/accelerated]</span>."	"The <u>dissolution reaction</u> of a compound <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>cannot</i></span> be entirely stopped due to the <b>common ion effect</b> - <i>only</i> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">slowed</span>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Having a large excess of OH<sup>-</sup> in solution will push the reaction to favor the reactants, but the dissolution will still continue (albiet very slowly).</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-49fb594893035fd40693d9eed8848a24305c6dd3.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-168ec39326798c0f6f0115640d0930f6ed06beb9.jpg""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;K&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;sub&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;sp&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;b&gt;&#x28;solubility&#x20;constant&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[K<sub>sp</sub>/ion product]</span>&nbsp;<i>increases </i>for <u>non-gases</u> when temperature is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increased</i></span>.<br></div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>K</b><sub style=""font-weight: bold;"">sp </sub><b>(solubility constant)</b></span>&nbsp;<i>increases </i>for <u>non-gases</u> when temperature is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increased</i></span>.<br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Since most <u>non-gas</u> substances are <b>endothermic</b> when they dissolve.</div><div>- Even though Ksp is a constant, its magnitude can change from changes in temperature.</div><div><br></div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""></div>"
"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;K&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;&lt;sub&#x20;style&#x3D;&quot;font&#x2D;weight&#x3A;&#x20;bold&#x3B;&quot;&gt;sp&#x20;&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&lt;b&gt;&#x28;solubility&#x20;constant&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[K<sub>sp</sub>/ion product]</span>&nbsp;<i>increases </i>for <u>gases</u> when temperature is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreased</i></span>.</div>"	"<div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><b>K</b><sub style=""font-weight: bold;"">sp </sub><b>(solubility constant)</b></span>&nbsp;<i>increases </i>for <u>gases</u> when temperature is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decreased</i></span>.</div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...since <u>gas</u> substances are <b>exothermic</b> when they dissolve (exothermic is favored at low temperatures).</div><div><br></div><img src=""a8d2a8b39de3a1d0836e4fe577cd5f1b.png""></div>"
"The magnitude of the <b>solubility product constant (K<sub>sp</sub>)</b> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;can&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[can/cannot]</span> change if <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>temperature</u></span> or <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>pressure</u></span> are changed." <img src=""paste-8653ce5e1c4569b8c5d1de1855e5b4ad6beae124.jpg""></div>"
"Strong acids/bases <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;do&#x20;not&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[do/do not]</span> <u><i>significantly</i> change the pH</u> of <b>buffer solutions</b> unless it's added in <i>excessive</i> quantities."	"Strong acids/bases <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>do not</i></span> <u><i>significantly</i> change the pH</u> of <b>buffer solutions</b> unless it's added in <i>excessive</i> quantities.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- The solution's pH <u>does change</u>, but it's to a much more <u>minor</u> degree (less than 0.1 pH unit).</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-07e354d4f50775b3b856a04dc3ed3baa9c3983d6.jpg""></div>"
"In an acid-base titration, <i>fully</i> <u>neutralizing</u> a polyprotic acid like H<sub>3</sub>PO<sub>4</sub> requires <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""3"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> NaOH equivalences."	"In an acid-base titration, <i>fully</i> <u>neutralizing</u> a polyprotic acid like H<sub>3</sub>PO<sub>4</sub> requires <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">3</span> NaOH equivalences.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>For each H<sup>+</sup> equivalent, 1 NaOH is needed.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f9ef074c43c66b80fb1753d137eaf1df162d8f80.jpg""></div>"
"In an acid-base titration, <i>fully</i> <u>neutralizing</u> a polyprotic acid like H<sub>2</sub>PO<sub>4</sub><sup>-</sup> requires <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""2"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> NaOH equivalences."	"In an acid-base titration, <i>fully</i> <u>neutralizing</u> a polyprotic acid like H<sub>2</sub>PO<sub>4</sub><sup>-</sup> requires <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">2</span> NaOH equivalences.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>For each H<sup>+</sup> equivalent, 1 NaOH is needed.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f9ef074c43c66b80fb1753d137eaf1df162d8f80.jpg""></div>"
"In an acid-base titration, <i>fully</i> <u>neutralizing</u> an acid like HPO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup> requires <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""1"" data-ordinal=""1"">[#]</span> NaOH equivalence."	"In an acid-base titration, <i>fully</i> <u>neutralizing</u> an acid like HPO<sub>4</sub><sup>2-</sup> requires <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">1</span> NaOH equivalence.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>For each H<sup>+</sup> equivalent, 1 NaOH is needed.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-f9ef074c43c66b80fb1753d137eaf1df162d8f80.jpg""></div>"
"An acid is in its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;protonated&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[protonated/deprotonated]</span> form <i>before</i> the pH reaches its <b>pK<sub>a</sub></b>."	"An acid is in its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>protonated</u></span> form <i>before</i> the pH reaches its <b>pK<sub>a</sub></b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>pK<sub>a</sub> signifies the point where it ""gives away"" an H<sup>+</sup>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-5250efec74e53b215b01074d1978b1dab5428a52.jpg""></div>"
"An acid is in its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;deprotonated&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[protonated/deprotonated]</span> form <i>after</i> its pH passes its <b>pK<sub>a</sub></b>."	"An acid is in its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>deprotonated</u></span> form <i>after</i> its pH passes its <b>pK<sub>a</sub></b>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- pK<sub>a</sub> signifies the point where it ""gives away"" an H<sup>+</sup>.</div><div>- When the pH is equal to its pK<sub>a</sub>, there is an equal concentration of acid to its conjugate base.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-5250efec74e53b215b01074d1978b1dab5428a52.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;solubility&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a compound in a <u>solvent</u>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">(mass of compound <i>dissolved</i>)</span> / <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">(100 g of solvent)</span></div>"	"<div>Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>solubility</b></span> of a compound in a <u>solvent</u>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">(mass of compound <i>dissolved</i>)</span> / <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">(100 g of solvent)</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- This can then be expressed in terms of <b>molarity (M)</b>.<div>- Shown as 100 mL of H<sub>2</sub>O below, but that's only because 100 mL of H<sub>2</sub>O is equivalent to 100 g of H<sub>2</sub>O.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-426a747fc9e4d89b92dc7871fbf96d8ab3ba19de.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>solubility</b></span> of a compound in a <u>solvent</u>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;mass&#x20;of&#x20;compound&#x20;&lt;i&gt;dissolved&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> / <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&#x28;100&#x20;g&#x20;of&#x20;solvent&#x29;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span></div>"	"<div>Equation to calculate the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>solubility</b></span> of a compound in a <u>solvent</u>:<br></div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">(mass of compound <i>dissolved</i>)</span> / <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2"">(100 g of solvent)</span></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

- This can then be expressed in terms of <b>molarity (M)</b>.<div>- Shown as 100 mL of H<sub>2</sub>O below, but that's only because 100 mL of H<sub>2</sub>O is equivalent to 100 g of H<sub>2</sub>O.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-426a747fc9e4d89b92dc7871fbf96d8ab3ba19de.jpg""><br></div></div>"

"<div>The titration equation for <u>polyprotic acids</u> (ex: H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>) describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;first&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[first/second]</span> <b>equivalence point</b> when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1</span> H<sup>+</sup> equivalence is used for N<sub>a</sub>, and the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;second&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[first/second]</span> when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">2</span> H<sup>+</sup> equivalences are used for N<sub>a</sub>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ff5adfdcf7e250f83b29101499a3e16b2dc1d339.jpg"">"	"<div>The titration equation for <u>polyprotic acids</u> (ex: H<sub>2</sub>CO<sub>3</sub>) describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>first</i></span> <b>equivalence point</b> when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">1</span> H<sup>+</sup> equivalence is used for N<sub>a</sub>, and the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>second</i></span> when <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">2</span> H<sup>+</sup> equivalences are used for N<sub>a</sub>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-ff5adfdcf7e250f83b29101499a3e16b2dc1d339.jpg"">

"When <i>charging</i> a rechargable battery, an <b>external emf source</b> is used to force the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>current (I)</u></span>&nbsp;to <i>reverse</i> its direction to travel from the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">negative</span>-end → <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">positive</span>-end.

<div>-&nbsp;<b>Specific heat</b>&nbsp;<b>(c)</b>&nbsp;is the&nbsp;<i>amount&nbsp;</i>of heat needed to raise 1 gram of a substance by 1 degree Celcius/Kelvin (stick with the same units for T).<br></div></div>"
"<u>Work</u> &amp; <u>heat</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;path&#x2F;process&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[state/process]</span> <b>functions</b>."	"<u>Work</u> &amp; <u>heat</u> are <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>path/process</b></span> <b>functions</b>.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><div>-&nbsp;<b>Left&nbsp;</b>= process function;&nbsp;<b>right&nbsp;</b>= state function.</div><div>- Path functions are work (W) and heat (Q).</div><div><br></div><img src=""statefnc.gif""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for a <i>non-gaseous</i> substance's <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;heat&#x20;capacity&#x20;&#x28;C&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-69fda4a09a7cb72a1d7bd3c1299aa65b4797245b.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for a <i>non-gaseous</i> substance's <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>heat capacity (C)</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-69fda4a09a7cb72a1d7bd3c1299aa65b4797245b.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>C</b> = heat capacity; <b>m</b> = mass; <b>c</b> = specific heat.<div>- Heat capacity (C) is the amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of some substance by 1°C/K.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-bf43fec4d3e4990741079381334ca70e0b6e39c7.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>Equation for a <i>non-gaseous</i> substance's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>heat capacity (C)</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;img&#x20;src&#x3D;&quot;paste&#x2D;69fda4a09a7cb72a1d7bd3c1299aa65b4797245b&#x2E;jpg&quot;&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span><br>"	"<div>Equation for a <i>non-gaseous</i> substance's <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>heat capacity (C)</b></span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-69fda4a09a7cb72a1d7bd3c1299aa65b4797245b.jpg""></span><br>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

-&nbsp;<b>C</b> = heat capacity; <b>m</b> = mass; <b>c</b> = specific heat.<div>- Heat capacity (C) is the amount of heat needed to raise the temperature of some substance by 1°C/K.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-bf43fec4d3e4990741079381334ca70e0b6e39c7.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"In order for two objects to be in <b>thermal equilibrium</b>, they <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;do&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[do/do not]</span> need to have the <u>same temperature</u>."	"In order for two objects to be in <b>thermal equilibrium</b>, they <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>do</i></span> need to have the <u>same temperature</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Thermal equilibrium can only be reached when the two objects are the same temperature (so heat doesn't flow between them).</div><div>- <u>Don't</u> confuse this with dynamic equilibrium!</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-bf43fec4d3e4990741079381334ca70e0b6e39c7.jpg""><br></div></div>"
"<div>In the equation for the <u><i>average</i> kinetic energy</u> of <b>gas</b> molecules, temperature is measured in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""Kelvin"" data-ordinal=""1"">[units]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2e65656b708a3a8ecf550172a14930c2.png"">"	"<div>In the equation for the <u><i>average</i> kinetic energy</u> of <b>gas</b> molecules, temperature is measured in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">Kelvin</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""2e65656b708a3a8ecf550172a14930c2.png"">

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div><b>V<sub>gas</sub></b> = rate of diffusion; <b>D</b> = diffusion coefficient; <b>A</b> = membrane's surface area; <b>ΔP</b> = pressure difference; <b>T</b> = thickness of the membrane.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-0e762febfe975786e9b2dd3ef957d45636f30b13.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-67a2d5d797bf32272d4f85272be7f70018bff8ef.jpg""></div>"
"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Henry's law</b></span>, showing how an <i>increase</i> in <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;partial&#x20;pressure&#x20;of&#x20;gases&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;&lt;i&gt;above&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&amp;nbsp&#x3B;the&#x20;liquid&#x20;surface"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span>&nbsp;results in <i>higher</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;gas&#x20;concentrations&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> in liquids over time:</div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg""></span></div></div>"	"<div>Equation for <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Henry's law</b></span>, showing how an <i>increase</i> in <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>partial pressure of gases</u>&nbsp;<i>above</i>&nbsp;the liquid surface</span>&nbsp;results in <i>higher</i>&nbsp;<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3""><u>gas concentrations</u></span> in liquids over time:</div><div><div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg""></span></div></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>-&nbsp;<b>C</b>&nbsp;= concentration of the gas in the blood;&nbsp;<b>k<sub>H</sub></b>&nbsp;= solubility constant for the specific gas;&nbsp;<b>P<sub>gas</sub></b>&nbsp;= partial pressure.<br></div><div>- Henry's law says the higher the vapor pressure exerted by gas molecules above the surface of a liquid, the higher the concentration of dissolved gas particles in that fluid (since they re-enter the fluid state).</div><div><br></div><img src=""5e9240207b2e46d2dc05d20eb83abbde.png""></div>"
"<div>In the equation for <u>Henry's law</u>, <b>C</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;concentration&#x20;of&#x20;gas&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;particles&#x20;in&#x20;the&#x20;&lt;i&gt;liquid&#x20;solution&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg"">"	"<div>In the equation for <u>Henry's law</u>, <b>C</b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>concentration of gas</u> particles in the <i>liquid solution</i></span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>C</b> = concentration of the gas particles in the liquid solution; <b>k<sub>H</sub></b> = Henry's constant (different for different gases); <b>P<sub>gas</sub></b> = partial pressure of the gas above the liquid surface.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2925a41e0b2c7e81e693de5102b8143c2b83af43.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In the equation for <u>Henry's law</u>, <b>P<sub>gas</sub></b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;partial&#x20;pressure&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;gas&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;&#x20;&lt;i&gt;above&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;liquid&#x27;s&#x20;surface"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg"">"	"<div>In the equation for <u>Henry's law</u>, <b>P<sub>gas</sub></b> is the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>partial pressure of the gas</u> <i>above</i> the liquid's surface</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>C</b> = concentration of the gas particles in the liquid solution; <b>k<sub>H</sub></b> = Henry's constant (different for different gases); <b>P<sub>gas</sub></b> = partial pressure of the gas above the liquid surface.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2925a41e0b2c7e81e693de5102b8143c2b83af43.jpg""></div>"
"According to <u>Henry's law</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;lower&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[lower/higher]</span>&nbsp;<b>partial pressures (P<sub>gas</sub>)</b> of a gas <i>above</i> a liquid surface means the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">concentration</span> of the gas is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> in the solution."	"According to <u>Henry's law</u>, <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span>&nbsp;<b>partial pressures (P<sub>gas</sub>)</b> of a gas <i>above</i> a liquid surface means the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">concentration</span> of the gas is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> in the solution.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because more of the gas particles <u>entered the liquid phase</u>.</div><div>- <b>C</b> = concentration of the gas particles in the liquid solution; <b>k<sub>H</sub></b> = Henry's constant (different for different gases); <b>P<sub>gas</sub></b> = partial pressure of the gas above the liquid surface.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2925a41e0b2c7e81e693de5102b8143c2b83af43.jpg""></div>"
"According to <u>Henry's law</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span>&nbsp;<b>partial pressures (P<sub>gas</sub>)</b> of a gas <i>above</i> a liquid surface means the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">concentration</span> of the gas is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;higher&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[lower/higher]</span> in the solution."	"According to <u>Henry's law</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span>&nbsp;<b>partial pressures (P<sub>gas</sub>)</b> of a gas <i>above</i> a liquid surface means the <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""3"">concentration</span> of the gas is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> in the solution.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because more of the gas particles <u>entered the liquid phase</u>.</div><div>- <b>C</b> = concentration of the gas particles in the liquid solution; <b>k<sub>H</sub></b> = Henry's constant (different for different gases); <b>P<sub>gas</sub></b> = partial pressure of the gas above the liquid surface.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2925a41e0b2c7e81e693de5102b8143c2b83af43.jpg""></div>"
"According to <u>Henry's law</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span>&nbsp;<b>partial pressures (P<sub>gas</sub>)</b> of a gas <i>above</i> a liquid surface means the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""concentration"" data-ordinal=""3"">[...]</span> of the gas is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> in the solution."	"According to <u>Henry's law</u>, <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>lower</i></span>&nbsp;<b>partial pressures (P<sub>gas</sub>)</b> of a gas <i>above</i> a liquid surface means the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""3"">concentration</span> of the gas is <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>higher</i></span> in the solution.

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>...because more of the gas particles <u>entered the liquid phase</u>.</div><div>- <b>C</b> = concentration of the gas particles in the liquid solution; <b>k<sub>H</sub></b> = Henry's constant (different for different gases); <b>P<sub>gas</sub></b> = partial pressure of the gas above the liquid surface.</div><div><br></div><div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg""><br></div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2925a41e0b2c7e81e693de5102b8143c2b83af43.jpg""></div>"
"<div>In Henry's law, <b>k<sub>H</sub></b> describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;equilibrium&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;concentration&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;gas"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg"">"	"<div>In Henry's law, <b>k<sub>H</sub></b> describes the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>equilibrium</i> concentration of the gas</span>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-e460506c803e9f187ea146980036c52932bbc7ad.jpg"">
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>C</b> = concentration of the gas particles in the liquid solution; <b>k<sub>H</sub></b> = Henry's constant (different for different gases); <b>P<sub>gas</sub></b> = partial pressure of the gas above the liquid surface.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2925a41e0b2c7e81e693de5102b8143c2b83af43.jpg""></div>"

"A half-filled or fully-filled <b>d orbital</b> (4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>10</sup>) is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;more&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[less/more]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;stable&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> than a half-filled or fully-filled<b> s orbital</b> (4s<sup>2</sup>)."	"A half-filled or fully-filled <b>d orbital</b> (4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>10</sup>) is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>more</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>stable</u></span> than a half-filled or fully-filled<b> s orbital</b> (4s<sup>2</sup>).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<img src=""Periodic_Table.png""></div>"
"A half-filled or fully-filled <b>s orbital</b> (4s<sup>2</sup>) is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;less&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[less/more]</span> <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;stable&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> than a half-filled or fully-filled<b> d orbital</b> (4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>10</sup>)."	"A half-filled or fully-filled <b>s orbital</b> (4s<sup>2</sup>) is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>less</i></span> <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>stable</u></span> than a half-filled or fully-filled<b> d orbital</b> (4s<sup>1</sup>3d<sup>10</sup>).
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>Filling the 3d orbital takes priority, which is why 4s<sup>2</sup> orbitals will donate an electron to make it happen.</div><div><br></div><img src=""Periodic_Table.png""></div>"
"<b>Paramagnetic</b> materials <i>always</i> have an <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;odd&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[even/odd]</span> <u>number of electrons</u>&nbsp;in their highest energy shell."	"<b>Paramagnetic</b> materials <i>always</i> have an <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>odd</i></span> <u>number of electrons</u>&nbsp;in their highest energy shell.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- <b>Paramagnetic</b> materials are characterized as having an <u>unpaied electron</u> that can orient its spin in the direction of a magnetic field.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-90fff3e9909b1446271768aec5572d428404edac.jpg""></div>"
"Equation that relates the <b>pK<sub>a</sub>/pK<sub>b</sub></b>&nbsp;of an acid/base to&nbsp;<b>pK<sub>a/b,conjugate</sub></b>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""pK&lt;sub&gt;a&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x2B;&#x20;pK&lt;sub&gt;b&#x2C;conjugate&lt;&#x2F;sub&gt;&#x20;&#x3D;&#x20;14"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span><br></div>"	"Equation that relates the <b>pK<sub>a</sub>/pK<sub>b</sub></b>&nbsp;of an acid/base to&nbsp;<b>pK<sub>a/b,conjugate</sub></b>:<div><br></div><div><span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">pK<sub>a</sub> + pK<sub>b,conjugate</sub> = 14</span><br></div>
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>In contrast, <b>K<sub>a</sub></b> and <b>K<sub>b,conjugate</sub></b>&nbsp;should be <u>multiplied</u> together to get 1 x 10<sup>-14</sup>.</div><div><br></div><img src=""paste-2675c3c6b8f0c23a6f9257a9c4ff6ab1b092f64a.jpg""></div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Stronger&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[weaker/stronger]</span> <b>acids</b> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> X-H <u>bond dissociation energies</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Stronger</b></span> <b>acids</b> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> X-H <u>bond dissociation energies</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Having a low bond dissociation energy means it's easier for the compound to give away.</div>- X-H bond represents the bond between an atom and its acidic hydrogen proton.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Stronger</b></span> <b>acids</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;low&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> X-H <u>bond dissociation energies</u>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Stronger</b></span> <b>acids</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>low</i></span> X-H <u>bond dissociation energies</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Having a low bond dissociation energy means it's easier for the compound to give away.</div>- X-H bond represents the bond between an atom and its acidic hydrogen proton.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;Weaker&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[weaker/stronger]</span> <b>acids</b> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> X-H <u>bond dissociation energies</u>."	"<span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Weaker</b></span> <b>acids</b> have <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> X-H <u>bond dissociation energies</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Having a high bond dissociation energy means it's harder for the compound to give away its acidic proton.</div>- X-H bond represents the bond between an atom and its acidic hydrogen proton.</div>"
"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Weaker</b></span> <b>acids</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;high&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[low/high]</span> X-H <u>bond dissociation energies</u>."	"<span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>Weaker</b></span> <b>acids</b> have <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><i>high</i></span> X-H <u>bond dissociation energies</u>.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Having a high bond dissociation energy means it's harder for the compound to give away its acidic proton.</div>- X-H bond represents the bond between an atom and its acidic hydrogen proton.</div>"
"<b>Impurities</b> in a compound will <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;increase&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increase/decrease]</span> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>boiling</u></span> point &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;decrease&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[increase/decrease]</span> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>melting</u></span> point."	"<b>Impurities</b> in a compound will <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increase</i></span> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>boiling</u></span> point &amp; <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decrease</i></span> its <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>melting</u></span> point.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Affects the solution's colligative properties...<div>- Solutes (impurities) → <b>boiling point elevation</b> &amp; <b>melting point depression</b>.</div><div>- <u>Boiling point elevation</u> occurs because solutes make it harder for vapor pressure to accumulate - requiring higher temperatures for boiling to be reached.</div><div>- <u>Melting point depression</u> happens because the crystal's lattice structure is disrupted, meaning you don't need as much heat as before to melt it.</div></div>"
"<b>Impurities</b> in a compound will <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increase</i></span> its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;boiling&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> point &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decrease</i></span> its <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;melting&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""2"">[...]</span> point."	"<b>Impurities</b> in a compound will <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>increase</i></span> its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>boiling</u></span> point &amp; <span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>decrease</i></span> its <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""2""><u>melting</u></span> point.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

Affects the solution's colligative properties...<div>- Solutes (impurities) → <b>boiling point elevation</b> &amp; <b>melting point depression</b>.</div><div>- <u>Boiling point elevation</u> occurs because solutes make it harder for vapor pressure to accumulate - requiring higher temperatures for boiling to be reached.</div><div>- <u>Melting point depression</u> happens because the crystal's lattice structure is disrupted, meaning you don't need as much heat as before to melt it.</div></div>"
"<b>Melting point depression</b> happens when solutes (impurities) are added to a solution, since it <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;disrupts&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;the&#x20;formation&#x20;of&#x20;the&#x20;lattice&#x20;structure"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> - allowing for melting at lower temperatures."	"<b>Melting point depression</b> happens when solutes (impurities) are added to a solution, since it <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>disrupts</i> the formation of the lattice structure</span> - allowing for melting at lower temperatures.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Detsabilizing the lattice structure (solid) makes it easier to melt into a liquid.</div>- This also explains why freezing point depression occurs... you need even lower temperatures to result in the formation of a lattice structure (since the solutes are making it harder).</div>"
"When <b>impurities</b> (solutes) are added to a solution and the&nbsp;<u>melting point</u>&nbsp;<i>decreases</i>, the <i>range</i> of temperatures for the melting point is <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;i&gt;larger&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[smaller/larger]</span> than originally before."	"When <b>impurities</b> (solutes) are added to a solution and the&nbsp;<u>melting point</u>&nbsp;<i>decreases</i>, the <i>range</i> of temperatures for the melting point is <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><i>larger</i></span> than originally before.
<hr>

<div style=""font-style:italic;color:gray;"">

<div>- Ex: 148° - 151° → 144° - 150°</div>- <b>Melting point depression</b> is when impurities (solutes) interfere with the lattice structure's formation.</div>"
"Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;volume&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;u&gt;cylinder&lt;&#x2F;u&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:<div><br><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">V = πr<sup>2</sup>L</span><br></div></div>"	"Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b>volume</b></span> of a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><u>cylinder</u></span>:<div><br><div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2"">V = πr<sup>2</sup>L</span><br></div></div>

"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""&lt;b&gt;&lt;i&gt;non&#x2D;standard&lt;&#x2F;i&gt;&#x20;free&#x20;energy&#x20;&#x28;ΔG&#x29;&lt;&#x2F;b&gt;"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span> of a reaction at a <span class=""cloze"" data-cloze=""non&#x2D;standard&#x20;concentration"" data-ordinal=""1"">[...]</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-cfa9b1a8a189f6225817ce297b3e43af6e5291cb.jpg""></span><br>"	"<div>Equation for the <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1""><b><i>non-standard</i> free energy (ΔG)</b></span> of a reaction at a <span class=""cloze"" data-ordinal=""1"">non-standard concentration</span>:</div><div><br></div><span class=""cloze-inactive"" data-ordinal=""2""><img src=""paste-cfa9b1a8a189f6225817ce297b3e43af6e5291cb.jpg""></span><br>
